name: Erika Kirk, Turning Point USA
subject: You knew Charlie’s voice. I knew his heart.
body:
| | |
---
Peter, I don't know how else to begin except with this:

**_I miss him. _**

Every breath. Every heartbeat. Every memory I now replay in my mind... I miss him.

My husband, Charlie Kirk, was the love of my life.

You may know him as a fighter for freedom. A warrior for truth. The founder of Turning Point USA. The host of a nationally syndicated radio show. A bold conservative voice that gave courage to a generation.

But to me... _He was just Charlie._
---
---
He was my best friend. The father of our two precious babies. The man who made me laugh when the world felt too heavy.

The man who, every single morning without fail, would look at me and ask: _" How can I love you better today?" _

That's who Charlie really was. _That 's who I fell in love with. _

**And I want to tell you that story, Peter. **

We met at what I thought was a job interview.

Charlie messaged me in August of 2018 -- very Charlie-like -- short, direct, and bold:

_" Hey, I need to meet with you. Urgently." _

I thought it was about a position with Turning Point. It wasn't.

He took me to Bill's Burgers in NYC. I thought I was about to be grilled for a role... and instead, I was grilled about Aristotle, theology, the Constitution, and how I saw my future.

Three and a half hours of deep conversation, and by the end of it, I still didn't know what to think.

**Then Charlie looked at me and said: **

_**" I'm not going to hire you. I'm going to date you." **_

I froze. I told him, "Maybe we should just be friends first."

He didn't skip a beat: _" I have enough friends." _

That was Charlie.

Clarity. Conviction. A heart that always knew exactly what it wanted. And completely unshakable when he knew what was right.

God knew I needed someone who would say, "I'm not playing games. I'm not going to play with your heart. I'm a loyal person. I'm a good person. I'm a God-fearing person. I want to date you."

**God just knew that I needed someone like that. And He said, "Here's Charlie." **

We fell fast -- and we fell deep.

It wasn't glamorous. Our lives were chaotic. Travel, pressure, early mornings, and late nights.

_But we understood each other... _

Charlie had found his mission. And I had found mine -- _to stand by his side. _
---
---
**He loved God. He loved me and our family. He loved America. He loved this movement. **

**Charlie also believed in you, Peter. **

He believed in the students who had never heard the truth before.

He believed in the greatness of America -- even when things were dark or felt hopeless to us all.

Charlie was a father, a husband, a leader, and a warrior.

But above all, Charlie was obedient...

Obedient to God. To his conscience. To the mission placed on his heart.

**Charlie and I didn 't just build a life together -- _we built a calling. _**

And now...

**I don 't just bear his calling -- I will lead. **

**I will lead the movement he built forward, in Charlie 's name and for God's glory. **
---
---
Before Charlie died, he often repeated one verse more than any other:
---
_**"And I heard the voice of the Lord saying, 'Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?' Then I said, 'Here I am! Send me.' " **_ _**~ Isaiah 6:8 ** _
---
**Charlie answered that call every day of his life. Now, it 's my turn. **

And if you're reading this -- _maybe it 's yours, too. _

We are all being called.

To serve. To love boldly. To stand. To fight for what matters.

Because Charlie's life was not taken in vain.

The evil that tried to silence him has only made his voice louder.

We will carry the torch, Peter.

Together.

And the mission Charlie started -- it will become greater than we ever imagined.

Because God is not done.

And neither are we.

With faith, love, strength, and unshakable hope...
---
---
**Erika Kirk**
CEO & Chair of the Board Turning Point USA [_**Support Turning Point USA**_](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/zmT4BAEuf_0_36Pt_qeUouGPU9W57ziAAa_Go-Opgn8YzNuvauqCj4OG0EWU0MZs446bXzl7T2AKb0I6TSVC-JbaN_nt9BGfN0ZrI2FiDfztF56W8kALjliVnlWvNzl5i-nbTymoJdsOBhhd7pojBqNJGaHqNxjW5KBh_mQyFFviCo-NheTIdDpTlaihaQgJECD316EHaGYB1gUe9puR9elS2R4xxUSSFEZ4Ny7zLZvHdPwQ_LJk_x_2EPmRh05dYwAQPv37LFGKmmnM6Zwwh3lUr_zuAFcqBibYMEurrMPagYFiKSjNuwP6Ex8sGiX4YFyIKeH1RBeqXBOzs11CMUyjTfKecDslkPWZaibPYjwfHx-Z1edHetI1aJFVijFrKjxEzn40V2-STOf2fZip9N7sG7rr-tmxKA-MY40Zpk_TJos9mNJ4H59uWnzXk8jrHr9A7QvPmsWCHMHQQVEN81MeExmvGsrN8UZXQYNjKU7HuWLp_hFrTCMG8uwO1KQVq3TGS5zYwbPJkPd7sBjIERM-KrJijJYW4Jw9WJogQnNasaffXPjCFsBhjguPRjVu4rCFJ3qQeVCWeN6Y8cFENl6QrjpTgV9pyNCi73E5khcLua6_G05UHs376W9S57aRcrDCYWY8era1vxUNfKXt7kl7LKb-1MkQJ0C8p5BmUFyZ9GZpTMo1yV2NMnsXgLlSeOgp5uglf2aqU8neL_eAMaKx0wbP4jPK-Zfui3C7Kya-i4S0PPhW7Hgp9L6rh48OoisnXvdIvlblOJajOJinfdjD8ZqjtrLkC8M_ORNOhe8h93P-mm4sreOYCkxlqzTu7aLnzpNbLwAL5rwDtdEpY30ZvoctcHw756SUDqHy0EAx4oRFXZsdofWQcFTQWnKHkjt7/uy29DhPFtIzA8h_iIg_wr1Dl4yi63BAb/13)
---
**P.S.** Peter, you may have seen this already, but I want to share this with you anyways...

Charlie loved witnessing excellence. It's why he loved sports so much.

He loved watching the best of the best perform at the level of greatness God intended for them.

Peter, that's why this photo... _One of the very last photos ever taken of Charlie... _

...Took the breath right out of my chest:
---
---
Because I know that look... _**I fell in love with that look.**_

He's in athlete mode. Locked in. Dialed. Focused.

It's how I first met him -- _driven, intense, humble, and joyful in the simplest moments. _

Just Charlie. _In his element. _

I remembered the early days of dating, us on a basketball court. No lights, no crowd, no noise -- just the sound of sneakers on pavement.

We bonded over how Jordan was the GOAT, and he told me about his basketball days in high school and I told him about my basketball days in college.

He wore a red bracelet on his wrist that read: _**" Work harder. Be better." **_

He never took it off. Until one day, a student came up to him after a speech and asked about it. Charlie slipped it off and gave it to them.

That was who he was. He saw greatness in others, _even when they couldn 't yet see it in themselves. _

**That 's what I see in this photo -- _Greatness. _**

**To me, this is Charlie 's "Jordan" photo. **

_Because this was his prime... _

_Because this was Charlie at the height of everything he lived for -- his purpose, his calling, his mission... _

He trained his whole life for this exact moment, the moment he'd be called home.

He gave it everything. Mind. Body. Soul.

And now, this image is permanently etched in time, held in my heart, as I admire his greatness. _Forever._

Peter, I want you to know -- his mission didn't end. It won't ever stop...

**Because it 's alive in me. It's alive in our children. It's alive in the millions he's inspired. **
** **
**And if you 're reading this, I know it's alive in you, too. **

Peter, Charlie's life -- it was so much bigger than politics...

It was about a man laying down his life, every single day, for something eternal.

And now, he's running his race where it really counts -- _where it never ends. _

**Charlie, I miss you. **

I pray you're having the most amazing time in Heaven.

And Charlie, baby, I promise I will never let your legacy slow or go silent.

I promise I'll make Turning Point USA the biggest thing that this nation has ever seen. I promise.

I love you, baby. Rest in the arms of our Lord as he blankets you with the words I know your heart _always_ strived to hear:

_**" Well done, my good and faithful servant." **_

You lived life well. You earned it.

**And I promise... **

**I 'll carry the torch you lit from here. _We 'll all carry it from here. _**
---
[**Help Me Carry the Torch That Charlie Lit**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/znoq27CQxy7vI0D6gdE8xN92tAqrawsvVRFajObby0fzr4OXU6neSD0XjJIVK12GeeWAZ07jPuLsyqDQUU4dAKmkaWPWVykmxDI8I4yx0g5SBcXmaUztBgNfZuul6QxJUVaPXHOs2_aWdc5JdHKU2OXe7EidaC5dPcHgDFj-0H0zOaeLTv1dm6SHdcz6p0R5Ipj1N4Nqy41Pb31QTDdQ36SOfKHJ2Rzvwccnt6VBOPC_ttxTEyEdt1uMUnJq6PaK6C37aAihuk6UNZTUuNezggLNwHBl-SN-Jg-ZKNMdvYxvzep5bk8B3eETWX2O_OmSJp2fGN8fIudIWdroMMnMLPK67swoM2NmWYfCoB8Ig7SSEwZKQPYUzeUJK8JJE9esl43QDhhVdwgtSrdWDtoeokuTIkYbjkzT-ilGI2MYEJVIKB5x9EUClg4z3QpGFxfT2WW8gR_N4NOUTg3LeJUOsI1c82eLKrZOIXWc4_ZJFp5bmpQYJpEqLgfBKA9wxuf39WkIxvru16rnVm5M40ywNBuH-UIDUytsMLQX2Mkt-8RjkwwZ98JzgI2RZ_mpXuhFmfwUQCHbSpF9rN9etbZGPaReTD8nuqooj9hZHniy6AE5h92-Tb12rkgvxuvgX5BZAWntcYs_lXWnMCKQOOX58DUDFZLkdKEuvP85zwkOM9VfJIyzt1s9cBFBUeJwgKeTOcHJqEviIci1pXh-BmYIvXsLAi_r6S2esmJGDV8UItqWfBXxN-Qfy6lVbSQtTiPiV0pYmf7pAPEj2awskieHT7nhnB2917GOHiICengVazFZ53ZCNE0CbDBROp_nNMz2xLl2-vsCN2GH9DcfLOvhhTMdXSUfF7k9Brtk6j-TjLE6QOrXbq8Xoq6BGX8vNv7IR1bR/wUZGX1sAVHn8ZN43pDWEORj65YcRojql/13)

name: Mike Pompeo
subject: Things are shifting fast

clean_body: I've been in situations where every decision carried enormous consequences. I know that the choices we make now -- both at home and abroad -- will shape the future for generations. That's why I want to hear directly from you. I've put together a quick set of questions that will help guide how we approach America's foreign policy priorities. Please take a moment to answer below:Should America strengthen ties with longtime allies, or invest more in new global partnerships? serious do you believe China's economic rise has become for U.S. interests? you think America should continue a strong military presence in the Middle East? it comes to North Korea, should diplomacy come first, or military readiness? would you describe today's U.S.-Israel relationship? tougher sanctions be imposed on Russia for its aggression in Ukraine? you support increasing U.S. forces in Eastern Europe to deter Russian aggression? do you view the current state of U.S. relations with NATO allies? you concerned about China's growing military activity in the South China Sea? additional thoughts you'd like to share with me? input isn't just valuable, it's essential. America's strategy should reflect the voice of her people, and that includes you. Thanks for stepping up, Mike Pompeo70th Secretary of State | | | | | PAID FOR BY CHAMPION AMERICAN VALUES. NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. 8136 Old Keene Mill Road Suite A300 Springfield, VA 22152 Pop Acta Media | First Look News PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: Erika Kirk, Turning Point USA
subject: This is the man I loved…
body:
| | |
---
Patriot, I don’t know how else to begin except with this:

** _I miss him._**

Every breath. Every heartbeat. Every memory I now replay in my mind... I miss him.

My husband, Charlie Kirk, was the love of my life.

You may know him as a fighter for freedom. A warrior for truth. The founder of Turning Point USA. The host of a nationally syndicated radio show. A bold conservative voice that gave courage to a generation.

But to me... _He was just Charlie._
---
---
He was my best friend. The father of our two precious babies. The man who made me laugh when the world felt too heavy.

The man who, every single morning without fail, would look at me and ask: _“How can I love you better today?”_

That’s who Charlie really was. _That’s who I fell in love with._

**And I want to tell you that story, Patriot.**

We met at what I thought was a job interview.

Charlie messaged me in August of 2018 — very Charlie-like — short, direct, and bold:

_“Hey, I need to meet with you. Urgently.”_

I thought it was about a position with Turning Point. It wasn’t.

He took me to Bill’s Burgers in NYC. I thought I was about to be grilled for a role... and instead, I was grilled about Aristotle, theology, the Constitution, and how I saw my future.

Three and a half hours of deep conversation, and by the end of it, I still didn’t know what to think.

**Then Charlie looked at me and said:**

_**“I’m not going to hire you. I’m going to date you.”**_

I froze. I told him, “Maybe we should just be friends first.”

He didn’t skip a beat: _“I have enough friends.”_

That was Charlie.

Clarity. Conviction. A heart that always knew exactly what it wanted. And completely unshakable when he knew what was right.

God knew I needed someone who would say, “I'm not playing games. I'm not going to play with your heart. I'm a loyal person. I'm a good person. I'm a God-fearing person. I want to date you.”

**God just knew that I needed someone like that. And He said, "Here's Charlie."**

We fell fast — and we fell deep.

It wasn’t glamorous. Our lives were chaotic. Travel, pressure, early mornings, and late nights.

_But we understood each other..._

Charlie had found his mission. And I had found mine — _to stand by his side._
---
---
**He loved God. He loved me and our family. He loved America. He loved this movement.**

**Charlie also believed in you, Patriot.**

He believed in the students who had never heard the truth before.

He believed in the greatness of America — even when things were dark or felt hopeless to us all.

Charlie was a father, a husband, a leader, and a warrior.

But above all, Charlie was obedient...

Obedient to God. To his conscience. To the mission placed on his heart.

**Charlie and I didn’t just build a life together — _we built a calling._**

And now...

**I don’t just bear his calling — I will lead.**

**I will lead the movement he built forward, in Charlie’s name and for God’s glory.**
---
---
Before Charlie died, he often repeated one verse more than any other:
---
_**"And I heard the voice of the Lord saying, 'Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?' Then I said, 'Here I am! Send me.'”**_ _**~ Isaiah 6:8** _
---
**Charlie answered that call every day of his life. Now, it’s my turn.**

And if you’re reading this — _maybe it’s yours, too._

We are all being called.

To serve. To love boldly. To stand. To fight for what matters.

Because Charlie’s life was not taken in vain.

The evil that tried to silence him has only made his voice louder.

We will carry the torch, Patriot.

Together.

And the mission Charlie started — it will become greater than we ever imagined.

Because God is not done.

And neither are we.

With faith, love, strength, and unshakable hope...
---
---
**Erika Kirk**
CEO & Chair of the Board Turning Point USA [_**Support Turning Point USA**_](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
**P.S.** Patriot, you may have seen this already, but I want to share this with you anyways...

Charlie loved witnessing excellence. It’s why he loved sports so much.

He loved watching the best of the best perform at the level of greatness God intended for them.

Patriot, that’s why this photo... _One of the very last photos ever taken of Charlie..._

...Took the breath right out of my chest:
---
---
Because I know that look... _**I fell in love with that look.**_

He’s in athlete mode. Locked in. Dialed. Focused.

It’s how I first met him — _driven, intense, humble, and joyful in the simplest moments._

Just Charlie. _In his element._

I remembered the early days of dating, us on a basketball court. No lights, no crowd, no noise — just the sound of sneakers on pavement.

We bonded over how Jordan was the GOAT, and he told me about his basketball days in high school and I told him about my basketball days in college.

He wore a red bracelet on his wrist that read: _**“Work harder. Be better.”**_

He never took it off. Until one day, a student came up to him after a speech and asked about it. Charlie slipped it off and gave it to them.

That was who he was. He saw greatness in others, _even when they couldn’t yet see it in themselves._

**That’s what I see in this photo — _Greatness._**

**To me, this is Charlie’s “Jordan” photo.**

_Because this was his prime..._

_Because this was Charlie at the height of everything he lived for — his purpose, his calling, his mission..._

He trained his whole life for this exact moment, the moment he’d be called home.

He gave it everything. Mind. Body. Soul.

And now, this image is permanently etched in time, held in my heart, as I admire his greatness. _Forever._

Patriot, I want you to know — his mission didn’t end. It won’t ever stop...

**Because it’s alive in me. It’s alive in our children. It’s alive in the millions he’s inspired.**
****
**And if you’re reading this, I know it’s alive in you, too.**

Patriot, Charlie’s life — it was so much bigger than politics...

It was about a man laying down his life, every single day, for something eternal.

And now, he’s running his race where it really counts — _where it never ends._

**Charlie, I miss you.**

I pray you’re having the most amazing time in Heaven.

And Charlie, baby, I promise I will never let your legacy slow or go silent.

I promise I'll make Turning Point USA the biggest thing that this nation has ever seen. I promise.

I love you, baby. Rest in the arms of our Lord as he blankets you with the words I know your heart _always_ strived to hear:

_**“Well done, my good and faithful servant.”**_

You lived life well. You earned it.

**And I promise...**

**I’ll carry the torch you lit from here._We’ll all carry it from here._**

clean_body: | | | Patriot, I don’t know how else to begin except with this: I miss him. Every breath. Every heartbeat. Every memory I now replay in my mind... I miss him. My husband, Charlie Kirk, was the love of my life. You may know him as a fighter for freedom. A warrior for truth. The founder of Turning Point USA. The host of a nationally syndicated radio show. A bold conservative voice that gave courage to a generation. But to me... He was just Charlie. He was my best friend. The father of our two precious babies. The man who made me laugh when the world felt too heavy. The man who, every single morning without fail, would look at me and ask: “How can I love you better today?” That’s who Charlie really was. That’s who I fell in love with. And I want to tell you that story, Patriot. We met at what I thought was a job interview. Charlie messaged me in August of 2018 — very Charlie-like — short, direct, and bold: “Hey, I need to meet with you. Urgently.” I thought it was about a position with Turning Point. It wasn’t. He took me to Bill’s Burgers in NYC. I thought I was about to be grilled for a role... and instead, I was grilled about Aristotle, theology, the Constitution, and how I saw my future. Three and a half hours of deep conversation, and by the end of it, I still didn’t know what to think. Then Charlie looked at me and said: “I’m not going to hire you. I’m going to date you.” I froze. I told him, “Maybe we should just be friends first.” He didn’t skip a beat: “I have enough friends.” That was Charlie. Clarity. Conviction. A heart that always knew exactly what it wanted. And completely unshakable when he knew what was right. God knew I needed someone who would say, “I'm not playing games. I'm not going to play with your heart. I'm a loyal person. I'm a good person. I'm a God-fearing person. I want to date you.” God just knew that I needed someone like that. And He said, "Here's Charlie." We fell fast — and we fell deep. It wasn’t glamorous. Our lives were chaotic. Travel, pressure, early mornings, and late nights. But we understood each other... Charlie had found his mission. And I had found mine — to stand by his side. He loved God. He loved me and our family. He loved America. He loved this movement. Charlie also believed in you, Patriot. He believed in the students who had never heard the truth before. He believed in the greatness of America — even when things were dark or felt hopeless to us all. Charlie was a father, a husband, a leader, and a warrior. But above all, Charlie was obedient... Obedient to God. To his conscience. To the mission placed on his heart. Charlie and I didn’t just build a life together — we built a calling. And now... I don’t just bear his calling — I will lead. I will lead the movement he built forward, in Charlie’s name and for God’s glory. Before Charlie died, he often repeated one verse more than any other: "And I heard the voice of the Lord saying, 'Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?' Then I said, 'Here I am! Send me.'” ~ Isaiah 6:8 Charlie answered that call every day of his life. Now, it’s my turn. And if you’re reading this — maybe it’s yours, too. We are all being called. To serve. To love boldly. To stand. To fight for what matters. Because Charlie’s life was not taken in vain. The evil that tried to silence him has only made his voice louder. We will carry the torch, Patriot. Together. And the mission Charlie started — it will become greater than we ever imagined. Because God is not done. And neither are we. With faith, love, strength, and unshakable hope... Erika Kirk CEO & Chair of the Board Turning Point USA P.S. Patriot, you may have seen this already, but I want to share this with you anyways... Charlie loved witnessing excellence. It’s why he loved sports so much. He loved watching the best of the best perform at the level of greatness God intended for them. Patriot, that’s why this photo... One of the very last photos ever taken of Charlie... ...Took the breath right out of my chest: Because I know that look... I fell in love with that look. He’s in athlete mode. Locked in. Dialed. Focused. It’s how I first met him — driven, intense, humble, and joyful in the simplest moments. Just Charlie. In his element. I remembered the early days of dating, us on a basketball court. No lights, no crowd, no noise — just the sound of sneakers on pavement. We bonded over how Jordan was the GOAT, and he told me about his basketball days in high school and I told him about my basketball days in college. He wore a red bracelet on his wrist that read: “Work harder. Be better.” He never took it off. Until one day, a student came up to him after a speech and asked about it. Charlie slipped it off and gave it to them. That was who he was. He saw greatness in others, even when they couldn’t yet see it in themselves. That’s what I see in this photo — Greatness. To me, this is Charlie’s “Jordan” photo. Because this was his prime... Because this was Charlie at the height of everything he lived for — his purpose, his calling, his mission... He trained his whole life for this exact moment, the moment he’d be called home. He gave it everything. Mind. Body. Soul. And now, this image is permanently etched in time, held in my heart, as I admire his greatness. Forever. Patriot, I want you to know — his mission didn’t end. It won’t ever stop... Because it’s alive in me. It’s alive in our children. It’s alive in the millions he’s inspired. ** And if you’re reading this, I know it’s alive in you, too. Patriot, Charlie’s life — it was so much bigger than politics... It was about a man laying down his life, every single day, for something eternal. And now, he’s running his race where it really counts — where it never ends. Charlie, I miss you. I pray you’re having the most amazing time in Heaven. And Charlie, baby, I promise I will never let your legacy slow or go silent. I promise I'll make Turning Point USA the biggest thing that this nation has ever seen. I promise. I love you, baby. Rest in the arms of our Lord as he blankets you with the words I know your heart always strived to hear: “Well done, my good and faithful servant.” You lived life well. You earned it. And I promise... I’ll carry the torch you lit from here.We’ll all carry it from here. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

body:
Have you heard of this?

Have you heard of this thing called a Labubu?[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z5r7h7ibdaifr81es4mhqn4c769fmikraioesk2g_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs)It's the latest "must-have" doll that's supposed to be all the rage among kids.
Maybe I'm out of the loop now, but I remember when toys used to be fun and cute, and now they remind me of my worst nightmare: [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zn7mgue1hu4hp39ta7tv3kqnu2lffkjuc6lcs1a8_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs)[America under the Radical Left's control. ](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z9e7g00s3nfs5me2613eehpr46nnkfqt5srhj3eg_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs)

body:

name: Mike Pompeo
subject: Things are shifting fast
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHAMPION AMERICAN VALUES
clean_body: I've been in situations where every decision carried enormous consequences. I know that the choices we make now -- both at home and abroad -- will shape the future for generations. That's why I want to hear directly from you. I've put together a quick set of questions that will help guide how we approach America's foreign policy priorities. Please take a moment to answer below:Should America strengthen ties with longtime allies, or invest more in new global partnerships? serious do you believe China's economic rise has become for U.S. interests? you think America should continue a strong military presence in the Middle East? it comes to North Korea, should diplomacy come first, or military readiness? would you describe today's U.S.-Israel relationship? tougher sanctions be imposed on Russia for its aggression in Ukraine? you support increasing U.S. forces in Eastern Europe to deter Russian aggression? do you view the current state of U.S. relations with NATO allies? you concerned about China's growing military activity in the South China Sea? additional thoughts you'd like to share with me? input isn't just valuable, it's essential. America's strategy should reflect the voice of her people, and that includes you. Thanks for stepping up, Mike Pompeo70th Secretary of State | | | | | PAID FOR BY CHAMPION AMERICAN VALUES. name: James O'Keefe
subject: Dark Nightmares
body:
I've been having nightmares recently that are dark beyond description. Terrifying. One occurred at 3:30 in the morning on the day that Charlie was killed. In one nightmare, a landline phone next to my bed rang. I answered, and on the other end was some demon, the most haunting voice I've heard in my life, saying, "I'm in your basement."

| | [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn1fc172vmsfb32gkbkf6kp5g0vn235gjklg6jvo_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs) [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn0h9h36ev4o0koeusmatcv0ihef2ovnkljjr5u0_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs) Dear Patriot,

I've been having nightmares recently that are dark beyond description. Terrifying. One occurred at 3:30 in the morning on the day that Charlie was killed.

In one nightmare, a landline phone next to my bed rang. I answered, and on the other end was **some demon** , the most haunting voice I've heard in my life, saying, "**I'm in your basement.** "

I sat up immediately, not knowing if it was real or a dream. My hands ran alongside my bed to feel the fabric in the darkness. It took some time to recognize that I was awake. I looked at my nightstand, and there was no phone.

Those who know much more about God and the Devil than I do say that this is an example of spiritual warfare. They tell me that it's meant to terrify me, to make me question myself and my own sanity. **These nightmares, this evil, come just before a story OMG will be breaking that I believe will expose evil in a way we've never seen before**.

The story was placed on hold due to the shock and grief we've all felt for Charlie Kirk. I used to run stories by Charlie to get his feedback. I relayed our next release to Charlie before he was shot. It was the last text Charlie Kirk ever sent me. I prefer to keep the message private, but he understood the significance of the story and that **it strikes at the heart of evil in our nation.**

I'm reluctant to "hype" stories anymore. That's not my intent. But I am asking for your help.

First, I'm humbly asking for your prayers for me and OMG's staff. If you're a believer, I don't need to say anything else, other than "thank you."

Secondly, [**your support is needed**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhinj2klitgp2ucks365evm5k6vj7c2p6t0irmcg_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs). [**Any amount that you can contribute is appreciated**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z03p8r8ki3am4fbquh35nr6sjjl0f4p3r8a9crd0_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs).

This story was one of our least expensive to produce, and you'll see why when it comes out. But it will open the floodgates of truth, and we need to be prepared for that. This story has the potential to change the world, but that's not up to me. OMG, reports the truth, the world decides. But I would be negligent in my duties if we're not prepared to deal with a flood of high-level whistleblowers after this release. And that's where your support is needed. [**Please, donate whatever you can today**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdo3qo9jo1r0uvp1dskp4oqeqhl6cqiegu7kbjq0_rp22sh2s8i6epb0slhm4oj5clh2bs).

Thank you again for all that you do and, as always, stay tuned.

In Truth, James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

---

Did you see this message, Peter?

Our endorsed candidate, Debra Shigley, is running for a special election in Georgia State Senate District 21. This is a jungle-style special election to fill a State Senate seat recently vacated by Brandon Beach, who was appointed by the Trump administration to serve as U.S. Treasurer.

In the primary last month, Debra was the only Democratic candidate competing against 6 Republican candidates, 5 of whom were white men. Her opponents were all proud conservative, MAGA Republicans running on cutting taxes, restricting access to abortion, and instituting harsh crackdowns on immigration – thanks to hard work and tons of time connecting with voters directly, Debra made it to the general election (!!!!!), which is happening TOMORROW!

Women in Georgia are already living under dangerous and scary abortion laws – and her opponent has vowed to restrict abortion access even further (we’re not sure how that would be possible...). We can’t let that happen. We must ensure she wins.

**We’ve said it before, and we’ll say it again: This is a highly flippable seat. That theory was proven when Debra gained nearly 40% of the vote against her 6 Republican opponents last month. **

Peter,
Donald Trump is motivated by just two things: cruelty and corruption. He grifts off Americans — and savors our suffering.
He’s used every second of his Presidency to rig the system for himself, and his buddies — cutting taxes for billionaires at the expense of childhood cancer research, affordable healthcare, and programs that millions of Americans rely on.
This is a boneheaded approach to government — and the worst part is we risk it seeping into Minnesota. We’ve got a bunch of Trump wannabes running for Governor here with plans to run Minnesota like Mar-a-Lago.
But, I promise you this: I’ll never stop fighting to protect this state we love from Trump’s corruption and cruelty, no matter which of his lackeys is peddling it.

body:
Here's how it went

| |

# [Back from our Supreme Court](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=174246113&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0MjQ2MTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTg1NTA2MjQsImV4cCI6MTc2MTE0MjYyNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9dJePAMpEmzl98UPoM29PRdSnHg1uvpTJMZvacUTpFY)
### Here's how it went
| | [Jeff Jackson](https://substack.com/@jeffjacksonnc)
---
| Sep 22
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jeffjacksonnc)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=174246113&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0MjQ2MTEzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1ODU1MDYyNCwiZXhwIjoxNzYxMTQyNjI0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTE0NTAyMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2Rc9-YVyaLNKe9zqhLgOXBHH8MqU3RQTBGOrHcwxnG8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=174246113&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0MjQ2MTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTg1NTA2MjQsImV4cCI6MTc2MTE0MjYyNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9dJePAMpEmzl98UPoM29PRdSnHg1uvpTJMZvacUTpFY&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=174246113&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0MjQ2MTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTg1NTA2MjQsImV4cCI6MTc2MTE0MjYyNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9dJePAMpEmzl98UPoM29PRdSnHg1uvpTJMZvacUTpFY)
---
| | [](https://substack..VVUhKB0dmBLj_UXoymefhs5GxTBlApqoKw2ZNgvkaIk?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/jeffjacksonnc/p/back-from-our-supreme-court?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Before we talk about our state Supreme Court:

Good news. We won a fight with the Office of Management and Budget, which had unlawfully attempted to defund AmeriCorps.
That move would have hit western NC especially hard. AmeriCorps ramped up their work after the hurricane and is coordinating a lot of the non-profit response.
They also fund a lot of Habitat for Humanity work in our state, and I'll be joining one of those projects out west next week. (I'll be doing some roof work, I'm told.)
More cases like this are pending. Updates soon.
* * *
### **Making our case in NC Supreme Court**
Last week, I appeared before our state Supreme Court on behalf of our office.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c06c84da-02f5-46b8-a521-bcfbde2be45d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is very unusual.
AGs almost never argue cases themselves. But a couple months ago, when my team told me about this one, I volunteered.
The facts: A man struck another man in the head with a pistol, then stomped on him, breaking his jaw and teeth and leaving him unconscious in the road at night.
That part is uncontested. He's convicted of that and not appealing.
But he was also convicted of robbing the victim - but the robbery occurred _after the victim lost consciousness._
So his argument, in short, is, "You can't prove I'm the one who robbed him because he was unconscious. It could have been someone else."
In response, my argument focused on four points:
* The opportunity for a different person to rob the unconscious victim was relatively narrow. (This was contested by defense and became a big disagreement.)

* The legal bar for these kinds of challenges is extremely high. My argument was that the jury should be allowed to infer that the person who pistol-whipped and stomped the victim was also the same person who robbed him.
* Several years prior, the defendant had pistol-whipped someone else and then robbed them - facts that are _almost identical_ to this case and something the jury was allowed to consider as a factor.
* If the defendant's argument were accepted, it could create an incentive for future robbers to knock victims out before stealing from them, and we don't want to create an incentive for violence.

You can watch the argument for yourself on YouTube **[here](https://substack.com/redirect/ae4fce1d-a2ea-4bc9-adea-3ff542639715?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
Three general observations about the experience:
1. Our state Supreme Court justices (we have seven) were highly engaged and asked thoughtful questions. I got some real pushback regarding how long the victim was unconscious, and the defense attorney got real pushback on the defendant's role in causing the unconsciousness. It was a good back-and-forth.

2. My preparation for this argument took a whole team. I may have been the one standing up there, but a lot of people came together at our office to help me think through the arguments, counter-arguments, case law, and likely questions. They also put me through a two-hour moot court the week before my argument. This is the kind of work attorneys in our office do every day, and it gave me a new appreciation for their dedication.

3. Frankly, the defense attorney did an outstanding job. It was her first time appearing before our state Supreme Court, but she was super quick and totally prepared. My whole team was impressed.

And to cap it off, Marisa took off work to watch, and we grabbed a quick photo at the end just before the next case was called:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8a67c2fa-b2ab-46b0-90f1-8201486e4163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **9/11 stair climb**
On September 11th, I joined a thousand people at UNC's football stadium for a stair climb led by UNC's ROTC, where I was once a cadet.
Together we climbed roughly 2,000 steps in remembrance of the firefighters who ascended the towers that day.
The event had an incredible turnout and a powerful spirit. They blasted music and kept the energy level high. My thanks to the cadets who organized it, and to everyone who joined us.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/79cba6aa-e194-4ea7-a800-9a3fd6b383ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Best,
Jeff

clean_body: Here's how it went | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Here's how it went | | | Sep 22 | | | | | | | | | | | | Before we talk about our state Supreme Court: Good news. We won a fight with the Office of Management and Budget, which had unlawfully attempted to defund AmeriCorps. That move would have hit western NC especially hard. AmeriCorps ramped up their work after the hurricane and is coordinating a lot of the non-profit response. They also fund a lot of Habitat for Humanity work in our state, and I'll be joining one of those projects out west next week. (I'll be doing some roof work, I'm told.) More cases like this are pending. Updates soon. Making our case in NC Supreme Court Last week, I appeared before our state Supreme Court on behalf of our office. | | ---|---|--- This is very unusual. AGs almost never argue cases themselves. But a couple months ago, when my team told me about this one, I volunteered. The facts: A man struck another man in the head with a pistol, then stomped on him, breaking his jaw and teeth and leaving him unconscious in the road at night. That part is uncontested. He's convicted of that and not appealing. But he was also convicted of robbing the victim - but the robbery occurred after the victim lost consciousness. So his argument, in short, is, "You can't prove I'm the one who robbed him because he was unconscious. It could have been someone else." In response, my argument focused on four points: The opportunity for a different person to rob the unconscious victim was relatively narrow. (This was contested by defense and became a big disagreement.) The legal bar for these kinds of challenges is extremely high. My argument was that the jury should be allowed to infer that the person who pistol-whipped and stomped the victim was also the same person who robbed him. Several years prior, the defendant had pistol-whipped someone else and then robbed them - facts that are almost identical to this case and something the jury was allowed to consider as a factor. If the defendant's argument were accepted, it could create an incentive for future robbers to knock victims out before stealing from them, and we don't want to create an incentive for violence. You can watch the argument for yourself on YouTube . Three general observations about the experience: 1. Our state Supreme Court justices (we have seven) were highly engaged and asked thoughtful questions. I got some real pushback regarding how long the victim was unconscious, and the defense attorney got real pushback on the defendant's role in causing the unconsciousness. It was a good back-and-forth. 2. My preparation for this argument took a whole team. I may have been the one standing up there, but a lot of people came together at our office to help me think through the arguments, counter-arguments, case law, and likely questions. They also put me through a two-hour moot court the week before my argument. This is the kind of work attorneys in our office do every day, and it gave me a new appreciation for their dedication. 3. Frankly, the defense attorney did an outstanding job. It was her first time appearing before our state Supreme Court, but she was super quick and totally prepared. My whole team was impressed. And to cap it off, Marisa took off work to watch, and we grabbed a quick photo at the end just before the next case was called: | | ---|---|--- 9/11 stair climb** On September 11th, I joined a thousand people at UNC's football stadium for a stair climb led by UNC's ROTC, where I was once a cadet. Together we climbed roughly 2,000 steps in remembrance of the firefighters who ascended the towers that day. The event had an incredible turnout and a powerful spirit. They blasted music and kept the energy level high. My thanks to the cadets who organized it, and to everyone who joined us. | | ---|---|--- Best, Jeff ? | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Jeff Jackson Jeff Jackson for Attorney General, P.O. # Trump and Elon bury the hatchet "For Charlie"
President Donald Trump and Elon Musk **[shook hands and spoke at Charlie Kirk's funeral service](https://substack.com/redirect/7b27b765-98d5-48e3-90dc-f7ed5fba251e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at State Farm Stadium in Arizona, where approximately 78,000 mourners gathered, with both posting images captioned "For Charlie" to mark their reunion.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7b27b765-98d5-48e3-90dc-f7ed5fba251e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Lip reader reveals Trump's warm overture:** According to lip reader Nicola Hickling, Trump initiated the conversation by asking "How are you doing?" and saying "I've heard you wanted to chat" before suggesting they "work out how to get back on track," reportedly adding "I've missed you" when Musk nodded in agreement.
* **First meeting since bitter public falling-out:** The pair hadn't been seen together since their alliance collapsed when Musk left his position as head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in late May and began criticizing the administration's "Big Beautiful Bill."
* **Feud involved threats and harsh rhetoric:** The breakdown saw Musk call for Trump's impeachment and a new political party to challenge the GOP, while Trump responded by threatening to cancel Musk's government contracts and accusing him of suffering from "Trump Derangement Syndrome."

* * *
# "I forgive him" Erika Kirk delivers moving sentiment at her husband's memorial
* **Massive attendance:** Around **[95,000 people gathered](https://substack.com/redirect/c8fdd33e-8453-42f1-9a62-dcc8ddf2521d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at State Farm Stadium in Glendale, Arizona, with attendees including President Trump, Vice President JD Vance, Tucker Carlson, Donald Trump Jr., and various Trump administration officials, including Elon Musk.
* **Erika Kirk's emotional forgiveness** : Kirk's widow Erika Kirk, who is now CEO of Turning Point USA, delivered one of the most emotional moments when she told the packed stadium **[she forgave her husband's accused assassin](https://substack.com/redirect/8160e183-361b-46da-bc85-ce0348979f7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , saying she found comfort in her faith despite witnessing his death.

"I forgive him, because it was what Christ did...The answer to hate is not hate."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6fcfdb3e-10e5-423d-9916-638cafd6e947?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **[Trump's speech](https://substack.com/redirect/77d66608-7d7f-4163-9a59-36e637adb5ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[: ](https://substack.com/redirect/77d66608-7d7f-4163-9a59-36e637adb5ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Trump called Kirk a "**[martyr](https://substack.com/redirect/365dffdd-5bfa-4f07-a0fa-ea650b4a5e22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** " for "American freedom" and referred to the suspected shooter as a "radicalized, cold-blooded monster." He also praised Kirk as "a missionary with a noble spirit and a great, great purpose. He did not hate his opponents" before calling Erika Kirk onstage for an emotional embrace as "America the Beautiful played.
* **Presidential Medal of Freedom announcement** : Trump also announced he would posthumously **[award Kirk the Presidential Medal of Freedom](https://substack.com/redirect/365dffdd-5bfa-4f07-a0fa-ea650b4a5e22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , the nation's highest civilian honor.
* The event also featured moving speeches from Vice President JD Vance, notably saying, "Charlie, he did so much more than tell the truth. He lived it...", Donald Trump Jr., Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and more.
* The memorial focused on Charlie Kirk's mission to bring forth Christian and family values to the forefront of American culture, while also inspiring younger generations to boldly step forth into the conservative movement.
* Full service recording:

[](https://substack.com/redirect/7cf90169-d65e-46be-8f0f-cab89e5789a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# Student suspended after MAGA hat worn to honor Charlie Kirk thrown in garbage
A California high school student was suspended after a confrontation with another student, **[reportedly sparked by his wearing a "Make America Great Again" hat](https://substack.com/redirect/3cbbc539-23b9-4f98-8072-e6524717abd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to honor slain conservative activist Charlie Kirk.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3cbbc539-23b9-4f98-8072-e6524717abd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Student wore MAGA hat to honor Charlie Kirk on 9/11:** Laguna Beach High School student Zach Hornstein said he and friends wore "Make America Great Again" hats on September 11 to honor Charlie Kirk, who was assassinated the previous day.
* **Hat theft sparked political argument and dual suspensions:** A female student grabbed Hornstein's hat and threw it in a trash can, leading to a political argument where profanity was used about President Trump, after which both students were suspended despite Hornstein saying they had "gotten over it and moved on."
* **Hornstein claims unfair punishment for unclear reasons:** The honor roll student, who had never been disciplined before, said he doesn't understand what he was suspended for, noting he only jokingly said "If you don't like America, Canada has open borders" during the dispute, and that he and the other student remain friends.
* **Family alleges political bias and plans appeal:** Hornstein's mother believes her son was held to a different standard due to his political beliefs, while the school district stated it doesn't discipline students for political opinions and is reviewing the incident under California law. The family is planning to appeal the suspension.

The way I look at it, we aren't just deciding Virginia's future this November.
_We're setting the tone for 2026, 2028, and beyond._ By embracing a politics that puts Virginians first — not a president, not a party; by drawing on the powerful conversations I've had with thousands of Virginians; and by laying out a common-sense agenda, starting with a plan to make all of our lives more affordable.
Our victory at the ballot box will pave the way for Democrats for years to come.
That's why far-right Republicans have spent tens of millions of dollars against me. It’s why Donald Trump is attacking me from inside the Oval Office. _And it's why meeting our fundraising goals is so important._
But as I learned working cases at CIA and crafting laws at the U.S. Capitol, you're only as good as the team around you.

body:
A Virginia case that shows how policy hits home
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01K5RWZ6KDYCC9T0GRRD3AZ302_0)

| | | | Hey Peter, Two months after a major policy bill became law, a Virginia provider is closing three rural clinics and says the law is part of the reason. Local families, candidates, and health experts are weighing what the changes could mean for coverage and access, and how states might respond. What is happening now and what comes next? clean_body: To keep Montana’s MUST-WIN Senate seat in Republican hands, we need to hit every fundraising target! | Fellow American, I’ll be blunt. To keep Montana’s MUST-WIN Senate seat in Republican hands, we need to hit every fundraising target! If we start missing our fundraising deadlines, we’ll have to start slashing key components of our campaign infrastructure. Right now, we’re in serious danger of falling short of our upcoming End of Quarter fundraising objective by just a few dollars. In a race as contested as the one we’re facing, we need every part of our campaign running at full speed now through Election Day. I know it doesn’t seem like a lot, but donations from patriots like you across the country add up fast. I don’t have the luxury of leaning on coastal elites and Leftist megadonors to keep my campaign running. Your support is the lifeblood of my campaign’s fight to keep this Senate seat and our Senate Majority in Republican hands. For America, Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

name: Navy SEAL Adam Schwarze
subject: We all need to be a bit more like Charlie Kirk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
As a former Navy SEAL, this isn’t the first battlefield death that I’ve seen - but it may be the most poignant | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=9a37b5829c884812b7a356635b68868c&_e=CiaNAIj3LJW9CW7jfzTtpmiTKMFHgxNXNz0C_ydUA386EwhnIiiIh63LmPge4ICHWRtChit7cxCuIU_ov4v3ZuG7bqJVR8V1IvtTG51pIaQtqjrMUS_rwDcNRlflAi9y-DfNhI_3ZLvD5teBQUU5NSz9o6Q_pco9mu9o-6JIDPnFCr2XkjaMhRnVnwP0viPlOmHraJ99t92lxC7_NPBjBZE2QiIwEWEbFy_tdECyr3oGVXKsiPxMbiJeUsNke7YNRIhXwN595l26_sM7QerIwRNWNWLnNqis-YTA4jzJqcAtay4YHr0fY98jNmPC6dOE2I9iIgwVJR6geO3VuclPpw_sMXaOtNqOsXO5_rCPJAUP5voCJHmVstnyWGsmVHnsNbSVmkP-pDfrTS3mHnsb88hLXyzFhr2Y2Q5uC_FjRfjSmPnzMHV96MaZHyTBTtnFuPhkT9-9_YEu6NgWj6gleXUoyFOBnJyWgl7BnVfgz0ZNBcSPECPVRlXHsOZqrX6Iwp0iInbrFNCL2UTnYNsr8qgo0hVKgXlzYm4N5QU3vAAVs1ZJoPNi4izHjV3JwkAZZir-kUmRjmeW75y_xKQug0uvt4W1GrKdZqjIWfvM1y4Uu0zViPRfOIaXUFlyJR5uNOsQ0smfOOhKBspBYBKIwP8ppgqcPA6J-RTeHYu1tCzft-DYkTGr9h0vzLuyZOKRwVQh3J56E-mQCVrgSbzQIa9szkU7WzTjwdQTTJME29NwEURmOgba8Jnwy9P51hpp_-yHbB2ntOozdZ5KWKAy6iaSBFlVs1fZ5L1mT9R4nd4YggYePG0Yt3GcSjbG3C6W)

Peter,

The nation mourns the tragic assassination of Charlie Kirk, gunned down on September 10, 2025, at Utah Valley University for daring to speak truth to power. His fearless voice—championing freedom, faith, and the American Dream—was silenced in a brazen act of political violence that has shaken us all to our core. Charlie fought hate with educated debate, not guns, using sharp wit and unyielding principle to challenge radical agendas. **Yet, he was punished with a bullet for his courage to speak.**

My name is Adam Schwarze. **I’m a former Navy SEAL running for the United States Senate in Minnesota, and Charlie Kirk is an inspiration to me.** Over the course of my career, I’ve met some very special men and women willing to put their lives on the line for God and country in some of the most inhospitable places imaginable.

**_I never had the pleasure of meeting Charlier Kirk, but as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I can say with authority that he has to be one of the bravest individuals ever to champion the values of the United States of America._**

Charlie’s candor, professionalism, and respect for everyone he encountered will stick with me as I work to earn the support of every Minnesotan voter during this campaign. **Charlie’s life should serve as an example to my opponents on the other side of the aisle on how to treat political opponents and people who share different viewpoints, with respect.**

I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11, served in over 70 countries, and fought chaos and extremism. I know what it takes to serve something bigger than yourself—just like Charlie did with every debate, every speech, every stand he took. This Senate race is my most challenging mission yet. Minnesota hasn’t gone red since 1976. I’m the underdog. _**[But with your support, we can honor Charlie’s legacy by stopping the radicals who enabled this tragedy](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=9a37b5829c884812b7a356635b68868c&_e=CiaNAIj3LJW9CW7jfzTtpmiTKMFHgxNXNz0C_ydUA386EwhnIiiIh63LmPge4ICHWRtChit7cxCuIU_ov4v3ZuG7bqJVR8V1IvtTG51pIaQtqjrMUS_rwDcNRlflAi9y-DfNhI_3ZLvD5teBQUU5NSz9o6Q_pco9mu9o-6JIDPnFCr2XkjaMhRnVnwP0viPlOmHraJ99t92lxC7_NPBjBZE2QiIwEWEbFy_tdECyr3oGVXKsiPxMbiJeUsNke7YNRIhXwN595l26_sM7QerIwRNWNWLnNqis-YTA4jzJqcCC-aqjdlJicKgUGxPI2zWmpHp34wmJVi8GPE0PW4Yq32EpNOiyxx7eKJqGluVGEhZb5qBigP1zJa8QfwzZ2BbAHQxPkBzuTv7d2pthb1xAJ2O3pNtFVDccEr9uRAR2VgGSZHe4YNqG3GSMUUf86tBnAnAZotUtzaPFoW7FudXytUbpdzqxoXoegmbMYjHEz7a3i-TnMmZ_yDqfOfAlbdWHOYaYVWrWEfN5HbTI97oTunXiaMfzR163frI5y6PN35OgIqT1DK0AZCLhiQbHXTGOzra44ED0fTJcikUnh_5l1SwsL3BpeFL7rJfWM65QfPMSbfkt2R5TSgE21JX0x4Xpx9IrXjR4sIvDKhEtMRE8GhmqNcl7Ls2flQQ3bOjoqlnBh9SAWKGRf5EnOSPmlFfbBFebIuqdTdxpk_brrqQqc7MRWUG0PVQ8zgd162ObJBhIeOaL6yi9l1-Jiux1hDbY8XXauxopPGHw8JG6iR8m33oGvG5FIBHGxAheCmBNvADL4WRcwptKkQB1AKESM9-p)**_.

clean_body: As a former Navy SEAL, this isn’t the first battlefield death that I’ve seen - but it may be the most poignant | Peter, The nation mourns the tragic assassination of Charlie Kirk, gunned down on September 10, 2025, at Utah Valley University for daring to speak truth to power. His fearless voice—championing freedom, faith, and the American Dream—was silenced in a brazen act of political violence that has shaken us all to our core. Charlie fought hate with educated debate, not guns, using sharp wit and unyielding principle to challenge radical agendas. Yet, he was punished with a bullet for his courage to speak. My name is Adam Schwarze. I’m a former Navy SEAL running for the United States Senate in Minnesota, and Charlie Kirk is an inspiration to me. Over the course of my career, I’ve met some very special men and women willing to put their lives on the line for God and country in some of the most inhospitable places imaginable. I never had the pleasure of meeting Charlier Kirk, but as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I can say with authority that he has to be one of the bravest individuals ever to champion the values of the United States of America. Charlie’s candor, professionalism, and respect for everyone he encountered will stick with me as I work to earn the support of every Minnesotan voter during this campaign. Charlie’s life should serve as an example to my opponents on the other side of the aisle on how to treat political opponents and people who share different viewpoints, with respect. I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11, served in over 70 countries, and fought chaos and extremism. I know what it takes to serve something bigger than yourself—just like Charlie did with every debate, every speech, every stand he took. This Senate race is my most challenging mission yet. Minnesota hasn’t gone red since 1976. I’m the underdog. . Unfortunately, I’m running against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor, who, like her boss, shares a long history of inflammatory rhetoric. Days before Charlie’s untimely death, Tim Walz, speaking in front of constituents at a political rally, lamented that President Trump hadn’t yet died, making his scripted condolences for Charlie’s family ring all the more hollow as he failed to disavow the extremists celebrating this tragedy online. I thank God that President Trump defeated Kamala Harris last November.** Can you imagine a vice president with so little moral clarity that he would tolerate the hatred that led to Charlie’s murder? I can’t. Tim Walz and my opponent in this Senate race personify everything Charlie Kirk stood against: arrogance, deceit, and a progressive agenda that sees conservatives not as opponents, but as enemies to be crushed. Their party’s years of demonizing voices like Charlie’s set the stage for this violence. Their silence on the specifics of this tragedy speaks louder than their words. They both push to disarm law-abiding Minnesotans while radicals run rampant. They both pander with land acknowledgments, dividing us further. They both view Republicans as threats to be eliminated, just as Charlie was targeted for his beliefs. The woman in the photo below is my opponent, by the way. If they get their way, they’ll impose their warped vision on America, silencing patriots like Charlie Kirk forever—patriots who countered lies with reason, not violence. This fight isn’t just about Walz or Peggy Flanagan — it’s about the future Charlie Kirk died for. It’s about you, me, and everyone who believes in our nation’s founding values. They think we’re “buffoons.” They think we’ll stay silent. They think we’ll let Charlie’s murder fade. When elected to the United States Senate, I promise I won’t stay silent; I won’t let his memory or work fade into the history books. . Your contribution of $5, $10, or $25 today will fuel our fight to keep Charlie’s vision alive—building a Minnesota and an America where free speech is protected, not punished with bullets. You have a choice: let them sweep this under the rug, or join me to ensure Charlie’s sacrifice galvanizes us to reclaim our values through the same fearless, reasoned debate he embodied. I’ve fought tough battles before. With you by my side, we’ll win this one—for Charlie, for America. null, Republicans poured nearly $80 MILLION into last year’s Senate race in Michigan – and MAGA Mike Rogers came within just 0.34% of winning.
Thankfully, he didn’t. But Rogers is running again, and **Republicans are ready to spend EVEN MORE to finally flip Michigan red.**
That means every single vote, and every single dollar, is going to matter in this race – and we need to build up our grassroots resources ASAP if we’re going to take on the GOP’s billionaire buddies and win.
**That’s why it’s so important to reach our daily fundraising goals.[With experts predicting that Michigan will decide Senate control, will you chip in $5 or more now to keep this must-win battleground blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/5982?t=3&akid=883%2E49239%2Ek1lzfz)**

Haley Stevens for Senate HQ

Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. name: GIFFORDS
subject: Add your name: Demand President Trump fund gun violence programs
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team,
The events of the last month have made it clear: _Gun violence isn’t going away unless we act._
Here’s one way we can take action:
For years, the federal government has been a leader in funding research and programs to reduce gun violence. These programs have helped us reach the communities most impacted by violence and make it harder for dangerous people to access deadly weapons.
But since President Trump returned to office, we’ve seen unprecedented cuts and layoffs for those programs — to the tune of millions of dollars.
The reason? One Trump administration official said that they “no longer [serve] the program’s goals or agency.”
This is unacceptable. Stopping gun violence must be a priority.
**GIFFORDS is on the front lines working to fight back against these cuts and restore funding, but we need everybody to raise their voices.**
[**Please: Will you add your name to demand President Trump restore funding for gun violence prevention programs? Every dollar we can invest back into our communities will help save lives.**](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_gvpprograms_sep2025//?source=em250922-1-b&refcode2=10899.3935704.hdwy7k&akid=10899%2E3935704%2Ehdwy7k)
**[SIGN ON](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_gvpprograms_sep2025//?source=em250922-1-b&refcode2=10899.3935704.hdwy7k&akid=10899%2E3935704%2Ehdwy7k)**
— GIFFORDS

Please, take a second to read this...
As a victim of political violence, Gabby Giffords has led the fight to save more lives from gun violence for more than a decade, but she can’t do it alone.
If everyone reading this donated just $17, we'd have the resources to pass commonsense gun safety legislation across the country. But not everyone gives — making it even more important for people who care and understand the stakes of our struggle to pitch in what they can.
**If $17 seems worth it to you to change our gun laws in order to make our communities safer from gun violence, we're asking you to contribute to GIFFORDS PAC today.**
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/64041?t=1&akid=10899%2E3935704%2Ehdwy7k)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=10899%2E3935704%2Ehdwy7k) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

subject: Our questions for you:

If you knew that the same $20 would help Democrats mobilize volunteers, contact voters statewide, and persuade those who are still undecided, wouldn’t it feel like you’ve done something really important in the fight against Trump? **These questions are very real and urgent — and your contribution today will help power our work across Virginia to get Democrats elected this November and check Trump and his allies’ power statewide.** Our work relies on grassroots supporters just like you investing in organizing in states like Virginia and nationwide to ensure Democrats have the resources and people power we need for the fights ahead. name: Erika Kirk, Turning Point USA
subject: Here I am, Lord
body:
I will carry Charlie’s torch.
I don’t know how else to begin except with this:

[ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2frZpZZviAo2KTwPvsw_PwaiYC-rBw0nh-JXujqPNvJQnfS2hBMwMb9UXmX7Pi_gip2ZFpbkqMP4bIumGEZyTncwaKgQyDSAcOnsDSQlgOOYYAprYM-GVQdc-d4PvgoVj1J6A0btBxMK6Ll6knQ5V2lWzGlvN4nfts_6CkB-T9hM3t5yfyjzr8fu4UIfV-qRBN3Y2y_xJbowxMrhutId48dv7Tc98tkw6eCvurO1gOgEzySh7Eyo7jKrf2O21RiEccNPdWhD8h7rrPHnnZ4gtfzxeVVlYlyWsX5CSe59jyZ5vHTVkvI6ysUrgXuVJG523VNDK5DtTG74Daa8Rm3eFo20Sho_fJnrLxDZCwXK-xTsIYgHuWov-f2f7wRmmeP8nuXXbOEQYnMZicL_TlXzp1V4i3wMXI1n89hu1euGl_HLAY8lKe5i8IGmGqcmq1abfJ_gb5hTnmy7jnKONq5UnL9w%3D%3D)
Friend,

I don't know how else to begin except with this:

**_I miss him. _**

Every breath. Every heartbeat. Every memory I now replay in my mind... I miss him.

My husband, Charlie Kirk, was the love of my life.

You may know him as a fighter for freedom. A warrior for truth. The founder of Turning Point USA. The host of a nationally syndicated radio show. A bold conservative voice that gave courage to a generation.

But to me... _He was just Charlie._
[ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2f0aVSaG3qTq1xWIbJu3N1cGu1X5ArRrcUpH4_aZZrziZqBWYdCuX0RVCOJnQfC0xMwHkkXZhfx7p5I5OWiW3RjeiTj1Dqn7ZRb7nA18c5APcuM4WutxQSKSCRpfrqio5_zGjRAJIqHykr-NzoOs8upkZEnIQ32fDQOLhHgD1psvd_HiPrIOXqvomcUISO4qPSV0R_baxpeCN1k7kN1CPM3LmOqD6JHGzuapkt6fdhv0XTQYAheLsElh4OemAO69VV2T3xMEUqXhGzDYjL3A6mKP8654sq3LGxXJcNgZ4az5WVBwmZ00TO1UgCqRxd26I8TZ321_fs1JROtjn7sJnL1C0REUct_88qdR6M2xwBip23oV_rsaFhnAEr0V7Fj1DN5lMFttQKA8_2NOdF4l_aqGkg3ZvKf0gl3YXyo78R_fGMGaoM3PZV0KGpjHx6k3hKR9HJlL2tkSvO71siJpkEkQ%3D%3D)
He was my best friend. The father of our two precious babies. The man who made me laugh when the world felt too heavy.

The man who, every single morning without fail, would look at me and ask: _" How can I love you better today?" _

That's who Charlie really was. _That 's who I fell in love with. _

**And I want to tell you that story, Friend. **

We met at what I thought was a job interview.

Charlie messaged me in August of 2018 -- very Charlie-like -- short, direct, and bold:

_" Hey, I need to meet with you. Urgently." _

I thought it was about a position with Turning Point. It wasn't.

He took me to Bill's Burgers in NYC. I thought I was about to be grilled for a role... and instead, I was grilled about Aristotle, theology, the Constitution, and how I saw my future.

Three and a half hours of deep conversation, and by the end of it, I still didn't know what to think.

**Then Charlie looked at me and said: **

_**" I'm not going to hire you. I'm going to date you." **_

I froze. I told him, "Maybe we should just be friends first."

He didn't skip a beat: _" I have enough friends." _

That was Charlie.

Clarity. Conviction. A heart that always knew exactly what it wanted. And completely unshakable when he knew what was right.

God knew I needed someone who would say, "I'm not playing games. I'm not going to play with your heart. I'm a loyal person. I'm a good person. I'm a God-fearing person. I want to date you."

**God just knew that I needed someone like that. And He said, "Here's Charlie." **

We fell fast -- and we fell deep.

It wasn't glamorous. Our lives were chaotic. Travel, pressure, early mornings, and late nights.

_But we understood each other... _

Charlie had found his mission. And I had found mine -- _to stand by his side. _
[ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2fvMG-UF02k-nW5N4LzjVrscfODAAllcOc6RbtiHhiI7G1ApwjvXuhfUEy3SE8ggheWIJyRGSjCI97dmzs7cZ6j2CUlb4z_Z5-Sj90cqb35g5f-EGD-gtmGK9LRKCKBd-WR5q9-fAjwpb8Y2rih148UdBCADkWKh0DZaLor6FPCCjAIDKmmmVbQV3W7eMQYi8pi4OEwDnISHd4lzO14e_yEmlVo2QbF7LqYM_eYiYuMQiTnq35ETxm6SsYVfETiiULrLELwE_rWqHlcapdOnBpQwtjTAE3IgTYrqZBLZ6Zwoia8QgCfLzrdoq78nhBVHUqLaH-ZZrTnRam3miYOYTMdJFXkxZBXRaDpI2f2xVky9MjpcMw-2hWz-AkmLjGIiBpI0xLyPbvKhHqjeOjYzkIqHSgclfE4ZBryM-cNok4RBe8U-ixZGHWCcihuXn6S5ml7TDB52J8vvH1W9JCroXQEg%3D%3D)
**He loved God. He loved me and our family. He loved America. He loved this movement. **

**Charlie also believed in you, Friend. **

He believed in the students who had never heard the truth before.

He believed in the greatness of America -- even when things were dark or felt hopeless to us all.

Charlie was a father, a husband, a leader, and a warrior.

But above all, Charlie was obedient...

Obedient to God. To his conscience. To the mission placed on his heart.

**Charlie and I didn 't just build a life together -- _we built a calling. _**

And now...

**I don 't just bear his calling -- I will lead. **

**I will lead the movement he built forward, in Charlie 's name and for God's glory. **
[ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2fTJWs_qM7Fh_FxSVtW84B0HAqQyyc6yfHmWlCsSKpzsiQhtl6yXLKaRqycXqEEC-kFCnjKdSNslnUSy-1rZAjxH-lrNCy9ubSYv3aBLxe5qkrwWEhoFmaZHJu39l2nroLBxkg3D50yJtaiXU7lT_taoJpW1q6K71QOg5OqgPx6M_cxQ0nBQjE9jtQFayNYFc8dRjNt5nmDWPE_dsb7xsz7CbJjwCWZ3O57A-XqJBe4859dHuoApPylAeWJsDzp8ig_GKmqy4hk7iMu3EAgl__mlqgn5AmBRLUQFAmGALn3AEfxmyxiaGvvRe09POITqBjbxHBP-dMMdRjRaxc7zyZ5lER07ABIBWnUUjPEAS7jC4v2DyJI_rLH_l3jGRvbkc7s_Dyi7f_szv2JriUJGyLummrc_IzfNNw4khY0oXN4uGhqcjSrIvAR5K8K-FU3skVznaG20oHSeKqXeNbk9nb7g%3D%3D)
Before Charlie died, he often repeated one verse more than any other:
_**"And I heard the voice of the Lord saying, 'Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?' Then I said, 'Here I am! Send me.' " **_

_**~ Isaiah 6:8 ** _
**Charlie answered that call every day of his life. Now, it 's my turn. **

And if you're reading this -- _maybe it 's yours, too. _

We are all being called.

To serve. To love boldly. To stand. To fight for what matters.

Because Charlie's life was not taken in vain.

The evil that tried to silence him has only made his voice louder.

We will carry the torch, Friend.

Together.

And the mission Charlie started -- it will become greater than we ever imagined.

Because God is not done.

And neither are we.

With faith, love, strength, and unshakable hope...
**Erika Kirk**
CEO & Chair of the Board
Turning Point USA

[ _**Support Turning Point USA**_ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2fbfcV9pB0k7-F0sID_4qKXNYohp9m3cIT5Qp2pD8dCjZkgO5Ptl_FnAWmHCrF9iAkYM5qX1pclDdHzyQ0Mg-FkjxpoYV1jfNbxQ-vAT9nYQ4siRvoVjlPvCiqfq2Prh6Cns3PrhKVLoASkZq7wSaKnNx4I_9h0hPd5aRQ0_ydLDwmwdMTkNMh5Bi9Ch8W8EDvBUjC4XGUxm-wz5WXZF25Tc8JW5TsMDRJaLSkNmvtRfDholwH4j7PbgzmXp7LeQe7eWfKsCQVaMd2qhvvdiwRIOYhvPicq-QyROzGgmpFYxkgpLJpF7PVou-I33DQBQBgSlQh9Px4Z7HDN-TFcGuqi1oWKPk-CUpPfweRsBdyfIxFY_e9pkyBg5hWOKHEHgwtUTxpPvYleyIFPY94QMzprbVxAg-39dtFjNRLYArbRH2PzoIhysOvtdb8B8sXxSHUiKZT2UPGkVr4B6ve-XdUwg%3D%3D)
**P.S.** Friend, you may have seen this already, but I want to share this with you anyways...

Charlie loved witnessing excellence. It's why he loved sports so much.

He loved watching the best of the best perform at the level of greatness God intended for them.

Friend, that's why this photo... _One of the very last photos ever taken of Charlie... _

...Took the breath right out of my chest:
[ ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2fSQen8NfR79m3Hp1ULXHW6bxOXhJcEuTI1qQXm1xAUfp61_Cy84yDsJTmjy5WUrilJDrlFO7GfjbpOG6bwQMaDOelIaMico1Sajg6hOvgJ5rcTNAl25IZuwWMq8hVBNEMq_s3UfFPt_KrOWAW4-HCsGmwa5fzGnUXAEFFM9paT9V9p1vy-jZ5-Qvd-dCufVJnY_i1keQ_UhyT_BMMFLBiNlFtCdiC11ju90eMs5VswLqV94SfLXy0aS4rPHnJYKjGkusAjPn_4ARKFWr8NPI7N3L1UP7FUp5WBaTE2HHHqFfZVFLIXVmOIOUs_2cqBFrUYGpNrFa1xJzcmjRxp-cxLWmK0-V6FyWoZH1ohqGEA8WGacv9SHR_xgtPp2uJlR5cES2KU35_EdJU1mxdu6Rj7wYYppfIMtoDAE-Ylvo56f2DepYxkovyXjNCFTJ-SC2o0rmvPE5iGAYwXUOd5B08pA%3D%3D)
Because I know that look... _**I fell in love with that look.**_

He's in athlete mode. Locked in. Dialed. Focused.

It's how I first met him -- _driven, intense, humble, and joyful in the simplest moments. _

Just Charlie. _In his element. _

I remembered the early days of dating, us on a basketball court. No lights, no crowd, no noise -- just the sound of sneakers on pavement.

We bonded over how Jordan was the GOAT, and he told me about his basketball days in high school and I told him about my basketball days in college.

He wore a red bracelet on his wrist that read: _**" Work harder. Be better." **_

He never took it off. Until one day, a student came up to him after a speech and asked about it. Charlie slipped it off and gave it to them.

That was who he was. He saw greatness in others, _even when they couldn 't yet see it in themselves. _

**That 's what I see in this photo -- _Greatness. _**

**To me, this is Charlie 's "Jordan" photo. **

_Because this was his prime... _

_Because this was Charlie at the height of everything he lived for -- his purpose, his calling, his mission... _

He trained his whole life for this exact moment, the moment he'd be called home.

He gave it everything. Mind. Body. Soul.

And now, this image is permanently etched in time, held in my heart, as I admire his greatness. _Forever._

Friend, I want you to know -- his mission didn't end. It won't ever stop...

**Because it 's alive in me. It's alive in our children. It's alive in the millions he's inspired. **
** **
**And if you 're reading this, I know it's alive in you, too. **

Friend, Charlie's life -- it was so much bigger than politics...

It was about a man laying down his life, every single day, for something eternal.

And now, he's running his race where it really counts -- _where it never ends. _

**Charlie, I miss you. **

I pray you're having the most amazing time in Heaven.

And Charlie, baby, I promise I will never let your legacy slow or go silent.

I promise I'll make Turning Point USA the biggest thing that this nation has ever seen. I promise.

I love you, baby. Rest in the arms of our Lord as he blankets you with the words I know your heart _always_ strived to hear:

_**" Well done, my good and faithful servant." **_

You lived life well. You earned it.

**And I promise... **

**I 'll carry the torch you lit from here. _We 'll all carry it from here. _**
[ **Help Me Carry the Torch That Charlie Lit** ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a2409e9a240549f68f6a579a9f4bb2a0&_e=kvY4ati_oM3YPwBnXUNS3TEzFb5ld4D6b2DoOZTkTk06L6ycL0fy9901FY0raZvkMdecIvm27uhpnGFIZMAY5QA5SJHCsZgwC0PNweS0P-d90zzOR2Xz1TNvJzoGpz3zpON3SEliGPrgX3m2xAGpomHXTfuQIl6j0XNICItJUaq7JZzcR9YKw9QACcA5VIViAeDKu5LLTac7Ux0AcUBJYzx3iRziMmK5d202d9Unl99r5BF32hTPhXvwqo2Evx2fVkRqWr-iXfm_76eKeSW-pJgotAGDaegHZ5wf3hHC6-W7Y7LY0ZPp6vZc0VSQhV5OO0AbnwD2Gos-AiDxXVrYV9abUENhtwfZjlZ0sJmlst86OU2MIEVgd4fvEMdfmbjPmPyzvNgSxXHeH8404uPx1aaAJhZZTHvd5f-Zcz6ZSKK_dBUjtkhtBcP-GzyGRwIqqtk3KG_KuExubHUpw0mYPzDVR25unozIQRPzOx6lRuhXSL3jrC4GTDq8-U2-T88h403vy5g6CT11gAw_Lh-KfeEGmpnh3vU4W9RN-FdEuzhnD6ICFf9s3fQ_XeUlQWHks5FhaM2UhLvdyJMn1anYEA1x1NX74sj0QtO4_mV9tB5VsCyDUi82Urh1OdHXXmwCsgNjV32240SpSyAlYhtkxVhGwIxjCYOH3EgX4B0iLj4BxMSoGxXEk2B2yr0ARuqqcwE1U7KrZwPVdxuz98e2sA%3D%3D)

clean_body: I will carry Charlie’s torch. I don’t know how else to begin except with this: Friend, I don't know how else to begin except with this: I miss him. Every breath. Every heartbeat. Every memory I now replay in my mind... I miss him. My husband, Charlie Kirk, was the love of my life. You may know him as a fighter for freedom. A warrior for truth. The founder of Turning Point USA. The host of a nationally syndicated radio show. A bold conservative voice that gave courage to a generation. But to me... He was just Charlie. He was my best friend. The father of our two precious babies. The man who made me laugh when the world felt too heavy. The man who, every single morning without fail, would look at me and ask: " How can I love you better today?" That's who Charlie really was. That 's who I fell in love with. And I want to tell you that story, Friend. We met at what I thought was a job interview. Charlie messaged me in August of 2018 -- very Charlie-like -- short, direct, and bold: " Hey, I need to meet with you. Urgently." I thought it was about a position with Turning Point. It wasn't. He took me to Bill's Burgers in NYC. I thought I was about to be grilled for a role... and instead, I was grilled about Aristotle, theology, the Constitution, and how I saw my future. Three and a half hours of deep conversation, and by the end of it, I still didn't know what to think. Then Charlie looked at me and said: " I'm not going to hire you. I'm going to date you." I froze. I told him, "Maybe we should just be friends first." He didn't skip a beat: " I have enough friends." That was Charlie. Clarity. Conviction. A heart that always knew exactly what it wanted. And completely unshakable when he knew what was right. God knew I needed someone who would say, "I'm not playing games. I'm not going to play with your heart. I'm a loyal person. I'm a good person. I'm a God-fearing person. I want to date you." God just knew that I needed someone like that. And He said, "Here's Charlie." We fell fast -- and we fell deep. It wasn't glamorous.
