name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: The Fall Fights Ahead
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501304250%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bThe%2bFall%2bFights%2bAhead%250115391.51032166.7465512.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501304250%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bThe%2bFall%2bFights%2bAhead%250115391.51032166.7465512.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501)
---|---
| | | **September 22, 2025** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
### Senate Update: The Fall Fights Ahead
Over the past eight months, Donald Trump and Republicans have made clear where their priorities lie. Instead of attempting to tackle rising costs, the Trump Administration is selling out working families to stuff the pockets of billionaires, special interests, and his own family.

Americans are suffering the consequences, and our democracy is fraying. But across the country, more and more people are seeing through their lies, standing up, and fighting back--for an economy where everyone can afford a decent quality of life and for a democracy that preserves our freedoms, values, and rights.

These are troubling, stormy, and downright terrifying times. Rising political violence has shocked our nation's conscience. The assassination of Charlie Kirk was a tragedy. Political violence has no place in our democracy. We must speak out for what we believe in vigorously, courageously, and peacefully.

But that's not what the Trump Administration is doing. The President could have used this moment to unite the country. Instead, [Donald Trump is weaponizing the murder to attack free speech](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fshorts%252fdaVT-Ay6ILQ%26redir_log%3d322879836382284&redir_log=1239137211503), including by using the FCC to try to drive Jimmy Kimmel off the air and to threaten others. [I'm fighting back with legislation to protect freedom of speech](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fyoutube.com%252fshorts%252ftITbs50lAKo%253fsi%253dXFB8LbXLP79Kxg0R%26redir_log%3d888799517877379&redir_log=406083568461622), and I will keep fighting until all our rights are secure.

[As I told patriots who marched from Philadelphia to Washington last week](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fyoutube.com%252fshorts%252fpgOZkWqKT_U%253fsi%253dgPLmba7vZnjp4C4e%26redir_log%3d985442768716394&redir_log=146666205431638), we must stand with those who stand up to Donald Trump's bullying and call out those who don't. In my remarks to the marchers, I called out ABC for caving to Trump when they didn't have to. I am glad ABC has reversed course and rehired Kimmel. Those who appease Trump only feed his appetite and put the rights of all of us at greater risk.

Here's the latest.
## The One Big Ugly Betrayal
Trump and Republicans are hoping that the American people don't learn the truth about what they call their "One Big Beautiful Bill." They jammed it through Congress so fast that many of them didn't even read it. Since so few of them seem to know the facts, I laid it out clearly on the [Senate Floor](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fwatch%253fv%253dDVOFEroHuGY%26redir_log%3d004251667478294&redir_log=704606424251520). You can learn more about how the bill will affect Marylanders and all Americans--including the inconvenient truths Republicans are trying to hide--by reading this [fact sheet on my website](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fthe-one-big-beautiful-betrayal%26redir_log%3d523902618709444&redir_log=188055780961655).

Did you know Republicans are now trying to rebrand their bill? That's because the American people are learning more and more about what's in it--and they don't like what they see. The Republicans don't want to call their law the "Big Beautiful Bill" anymore because the American people can tell that it's only "beautiful" if you're a billionaire or a very wealthy person who is getting a windfall tax break. To help pay for those tax giveaways to the richest of the rich, their bill slashes health care, food assistance, and energy programs that working families depend on. Millions will lose health insurance. Hospitals and nursing homes across the country will close. One study estimates that the bill's health care cuts could result in 51,000 preventable deaths every year. And they left in place a ticking time bomb: at the end of this year, many of the Affordable Care Act tax credits will expire. When that happens, not only will 4 million of our fellow Americans lose health care and become uninsured, but nearly 20 million Americans will see a huge spike in their premiums.

[I recently held a roundtable in Prince George's County](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.baltimoresun.com%252f2025%252f09%252f15%252fmarylanders-face-impacts-from-looming-health-cuts%252f%26redir_log%3d248736351052618&redir_log=167238511031870) to discuss these devastating cuts to our social safety net with service and health providers that see their impact each day. They know more than anyone the ways that this bill will hurt families.

And even with all these cuts, the bill still adds trillions to the national debt to pay for their tax cuts for billionaires.

Once again, Republicans in Congress did not have the guts to stand up to Donald Trump--they were his rubber stamps. They made clear where their values lie--supporting the ultra-rich at the expense of everyone else.

[This summer, Republicans in Congress also obeyed President Trump's demand to cut public investments that they had previously voted for, and he had signed into law](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dQueXcQO4gME&redir_log=19873805756293)--using what is known as the "rescissions process," _rescinding_ previously agreed-to funds. That bill eliminated what had been bipartisan funding for public television and public radio because Donald Trump doesn't like news stations that provide the facts or tell the truth. Their rescissions bill also kneecapped our foreign assistance efforts to combat public health risks and advance American interests abroad. Under Trump and Congressional Republicans, the billionaires and our foreign adversaries win; everyone else loses.

Now the Trump Administration is going even further, pursuing a lawless effort to cancel funding--that has been approved by Congress and signed into law--without even a vote in Congress. Specifically, Trump's Office of Management and Budget Director, Russell Vought, terminated $5 billion in Congressionally appropriated funding and said that the cancellation would stand even if Congress did not vote on it. This maneuver, called a "pocket rescission," is blatantly unconstitutional. As the Constitution makes clear and the independent, nonpartisan Government Accountability Office has reaffirmed, the President of the United States does not have the power to unilaterally cancel laws that are already on the books.
## The Looming Trump Shutdown
The next major fight before us is over keeping the federal government open. Congressional Republicans have surrendered their constitutional duties to Trump. They have become his rubber stamps. He is the only one calling the shots for Republicans, and he had publicly declared that he would not work with Democrats to fund the government. Only after we blocked his blank check House Republican bill in the Senate on Friday did he finally agree to meet with Democratic leaders to discuss the way forward.

I, for one, will not vote to give Donald Trump a blank check to pursue his extreme and lawless agenda. There are no adequate safeguards in the Republicans' proposal to prevent him from continuing to illegally withhold and transfer public funds at his whim. Without those guardrails, it's not worth the paper it's written on. Moreover, there is nothing to defuse the ticking time bomb he left on Americans' health care that will explode at the end of the year and result in millions of Americans losing access to care and huge spikes in premiums for others. Trump has refused to work with us to address these and other issues. He has chosen instead to send us barreling toward a shutdown. I explained all of this in the last few days--see more [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fyoutube.com%252fshorts%252f-U9ymKI6Fjc%253fsi%253dq_GS5jZIa1ehUBdr%26redir_log%3d731912761446388&redir_log=670267591061627).
## Combatting Trump's Authoritarian Overreach
Trump's quest for absolute power is corroding our judicial and democratic institutions. As discussed earlier, he is doubling down on his attacks on free speech. [He is also weaponizing the Department of Justice by firing officials for not bringing cases against his adversaries](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fx.com%252fChrisVanHollen%252fstatus%252f1969783633264165095%26redir_log%3d408634061856332&redir_log=723212624581559). He is creating a culture of fear throughout the government for anyone who presents facts that don't fit his political narrative. These pressures are corrupting our institutions.

His politicization of the National Guard is another abuse of power. I have worked successfully over the last few years to increase the presence of FBI, DEA, and ATF agents in Baltimore to work in coordination with Mayor Scott and local law enforcement. As a result of these and other measures taken in partnership with the Mayor and others, we have seen important progress in strengthening public safety. There is certainly more work ahead, and we always welcome additional federal resources to support local efforts, but unilaterally deploying the National Guard there -- as Trump has threatened -- is not the answer. The Posse Comitatus Act states--and a federal district court judge recently affirmed--that the president is barred from deploying the military for domestic law enforcement. Trump's real objective is clear: trying to normalize the idea that -- despite the law -- he can do whatever he wants with the military.

Beyond deploying the National Guard, Trump also partially took over DC's Metropolitan Police Department, and he tried to seize total control until a federal judge warned that she would block such a move. He has also diverted important federal law enforcement resources away from national priorities--like fighting drug kingpins and going after the nation's most wanted criminals--to engage in traffic stops in DC neighborhoods.

If Republicans wanted to address public safety in the District of Columbia, they wouldn't have blocked a billion dollars of the District's own local funding for police, firefighters, schools, and other essential services earlier this year. Senator Collins and I passed a measure in the Senate to allow DC to implement its plan to use its own tax monies for these purposes, but House Republicans have not taken up our bill.

Trump's actions in DC also highlight another issue--the lack of self-determination for the people who reside in our nation's capital. Since 2020, I've been working to advance legislation that would give DC control over its own National Guard and revoke the president's ability to federalize the DC police force. Ultimately, the answer is to give the people of the District of Columbia full voting representation in the House and Senate by granting them statehood. I am the Senate's lead sponsor of the bill to do that. It is a stain on our democracy that the people of DC, the capital of our country, with a larger population than two states, don't have full representation and voting rights in the House and Senate.
## Seeking the Truth and Fighting for Humanity in Israel, Gaza, and the West Bank
As I have long said, Israel was not only justified, but had a duty to its people, to defend itself against Hamas, which murdered over 1,200 people and seized over 250 hostages on October 7. We must not rest until every hostage comes home. There must be no more October 7s. But the extremist Netanyahu government has gone far beyond targeting Hamas to imposing collective punishment on all the people of Gaza.

The situation in Gaza has gone from horrible to hell on earth. As the Netanyahu government restricts food, [the people of Gaza are starving](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fwatch%253fv%253dHRGcn-6ewx4%2526t%253d0s%26redir_log%3d12533565895349&redir_log=867161877871563). This latest stage of food deprivation began on March 2, when the Netanyahu government imposed a total blockade for 78 days on all food and other humanitarian assistance entering Gaza. Then, Netanyahu pushed out the relatively effective, UN-backed aid delivery infrastructure and replaced it, in part, with the so-called Gaza Humanitarian Foundation (GHF), a private group backed by $30 million from the Trump Administration.

The head of the GHF quit immediately upon its launch because, as he said, "it is not possible to implement this plan while also strictly adhering to the humanitarian principles of humanity, neutrality, impartiality, and independence, which I will not abandon." The GHF was allowed to establish only four sites--three of them concentrated in southern Gaza and none in the north--to feed over 2 million people. That replaced the UN and World Food Program system that had previously served about 400 sites throughout Gaza.

Recently, I traveled to the Middle East with Senator Jeff Merkley, where we investigated these and other issues firsthand. We visited Israel, the West Bank, Jordan, and Egypt, including points along the border with Gaza. During our trip, we also spoke with government officials, local communities, international humanitarian aid organizations, U.S. personnel, civil society organizations, and more.

One of our first meetings of the trip took place in [Hostages Square](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fshorts%252fcQZQT5JbL2k%26redir_log%3d21307823882285&redir_log=961473821111498) in Tel Aviv, where we sat down with families whose loved ones were taken hostage by Hamas in their heinous terrorist attack on October 7. The families we spoke with all told us that Netanyahu has placed his own political survival over the survival of the hostages. We also visited [Kfar Aza](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fshorts%252fuBi54FiU7xg%26redir_log%3d040795719102288&redir_log=561523153391505), the site of one of the worst massacres on October 7. The thriving kibbutz we had visited before the war, in the summer of 2023, has become a much emptier and somber place. But the community remains strong, and a community leader there gave us the same message we received from other hostage families: end the war, return the hostages, and find a way for Israelis and Palestinians to live in peace with security, dignity, and human rights--including the right of self-determination--for all.

We also visited communities in the [West Bank](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fshorts%252fj0uirp92qBI%26redir_log%3d296560640645289&redir_log=075097599571508), where we discussed the increasing, ongoing violence by extremist settlers, including meeting with American citizens whose family members have been killed by the IDF and violent settlers. We also met with members of the Christian community of Taybeh, which was recently attacked by violent settlers. We closely examined the very broken system for the distribution of humanitarian aid to Gaza, including visiting the [Rafah](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252fshorts%252f_GWShyXesOA%26redir_log%3d790881003049292&redir_log=542121226661510) and [Kerem Shalom](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fyoutube.com%2fshorts%2f1YsGe0hrQQg%3fsi%3dHM-tN09jYZ-wmxnY&redir_log=394031715624390) border crossings into Gaza and the Jordan aid corridor.

What we learned firsthand reinforced the reports of devastation, starvation, and the Netanyahu government's use of hunger as a weapon of war as it pursues its openly stated current war aim of removing Palestinians from Gaza. We recently issued a full report on our findings, titled ["The Netanyahu Government is Implementing a Plan to Ethnically Cleanse Gaza of Palestinians. America is Complicit. The World Must Stop It."](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fimo%252fmedia%252fdoc%252fvan_hollen_merkley_report_following_2025_codel_to_gaza_border_israel_west_bank_jordan_and_egypt.pdf%26redir_log%3d807693016241043&redir_log=410167192992308)

I have been very deliberate in distinguishing between the actions of the extremist Netanyahu government and the people of Israel--in the same way that I believe Americans have a duty to stand up against the extremist actions of Donald Trump. We have a duty to ensure that our tax dollars do not support actions by the Netanyahu government that violate our laws and our values. That's why I have consistently [opposed the transfer of US-funded weapons for use in Gaza as the Netanyahu government conducts its campaign of ethnic cleansing](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dHRGcn-6ewx4&redir_log=539740887523292). America has leverage. We must use that leverage to demand adherence to American law and American values. We must demand a ceasefire, a return of all hostages, and a flood of humanitarian aid into Gaza NOW.
## Action in Our Communities
Over the last few months, I have visited communities across Maryland to discuss their priorities, to stand up to Trump's abuses, and speak with people about how, together, we're fighting back against these betrayals.

Senator Alsobrooks and Representatives Mfume, Ivey, Olszewski, Elfreth, and I attempted to visit the Baltimore ICE facility where the Trump Administration is holding people in inhumane conditions before working to deport them. [The first time we went, they blocked us from entering their facility](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fyoutu.be%252f2PFSssuxwgk%253fsi%253dx_K4OfsHQHqmLCPK%26redir_log%3d503466851763324&redir_log=696583205871549). We told them that denying us the opportunity to inspect the facility was a violation of the law, and I read that very provision in law to them. They later relented, and Representatives Mfume, Elfreth, and I returned to inspect the facility. The rooms are only equipped for 12-hour holds, but ICE is detaining people there for much longer. ICE would not let us speak to the detainees to learn directly from them about their experiences or conditions. If ICE had nothing to hide, they wouldn't be throwing up roadblocks at every point.

Here's the truth: Trump is not fixing our broken immigration system. He said his Administration would focus on the "worst of the worst," but--according to ICE's own data from this summer--84% of ICE's detainees pose no public safety risk. ICE has since removed that data from its dashboard, so the public can no longer see it. They want to hide what they're doing to people in ICE facilities from the American people; we can't let them get away with that.

Our immigration system is broken and needs to be fixed. That's why I support strong border security, removing the worst of the worst, and improving our legal immigration system. But we must say no to mass deportation, no to tearing families apart, no to concentration camps, and no to surrendering the right of due process. We will not snuff out Lady Liberty's flame.

[I also recently met with Erick Oribio](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fthebanner.com%252fpolitics-power%252fnational-politics%252fvan-hollen-trump-erick-oribio-prisoner-swap-QJWBUY5P7ZGR3BNRLNHBWLG23U%252f%253fschk%253d%2526rchk%253d%2526utm_source%253dThe%252bBaltimore%252bBanner%2526utm_campaign%253d2588164072-NL_PMSC_20250812_1737%2526utm_medium%253demail%2526utm_term%253d0_fed75856d2-2588164072-592437844%2526mc_cid%253d2588164072%2526mc_eid%253d40d34dcd12%26redir_log%3d152888107692160&redir_log=260773473773408), an American citizen and Baltimore resident, who the Venezuelan government wrongfully detained for seven months. I worked quietly with a bipartisan team of Senators, the nonprofit hostage advocacy group the Foley Foundation, and others to help secure his release. I'm grateful to the special envoy and our partners in that effort, and I will keep working for the liberty of EVERY American wrongfully detained.

[I'm also working with my Team Maryland colleagues to call on the IRS to expedite tax refunds for constituents facing financial hardship](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fnews%252fpress-releases%252fmaryland-delegation-members-call-on-irs-to-expedite-tax-refunds-for-constituents-facing-financial-hardship%26redir_log%3d854045765271121&redir_log=777779077442374). We've heard from Marylanders, both individual taxpayers and business owners, waiting years for refunds they're owed. The Trump Administration's mass staff cuts at the IRS have likely contributed to these processing delays.

On the Eastern Shore, I held a town hall with over 400 Marylanders at Chesapeake College. I took questions from residents on a range of topics, from the threats to our democracy to ways we work together for the Eastern Shore and our state. [You can watch the full town hall here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.youtube.com%252flive%252ffxbAZZapiTU%26redir_log%3d834180111392278&redir_log=280074099811495). From there, I spent time with our local leaders at the annual conference of the Maryland Association of Counties (MACo), joined the Salisbury Area and Greater Ocean City Chambers of Commerce for a forum to discuss Eastern Shore priorities, and also visited the [City of Crisfield](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fnews%252fpress-releases%252fphotos%252fvideo-van-hollen-views-impact-of-fema-cuts-on-city-of-crisfields-flood-mitigation-efforts-shares-update-on-bipartisan-effort-to-restore-funds%26redir_log%3d422320242421414&redir_log=924402364842681), where Trump canceled a $36 million FEMA grant designed to harden the community's resilience against floods that regularly cause damage to local residents' homes and businesses. [We are pushing, on a bipartisan basis, to restore this funding](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-tillis-murray-murkowski-lead-bipartisan-bicameral-letter-calling-on-dhs-to-reinstate-disaster-mitigation-program-critical-to-local-communities&redir_log=246716734481456).

I'm also continuing to support the recovery effort after the floods that devastated Allegany and Garrett Counties this spring. [Senator Alsobrooks, Representative Delaney, and I led federal Team Maryland in urging the Trump Administration to approve the State of Maryland's request for a presidential disaster declaration](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fnews%252fpress-releases%252fmaryland-delegation-urges-president-trump-to-approve-disaster-declaration-for-allegany-garrett-counties-following-destructive-floods%26redir_log%3d316780429041317&redir_log=898192863992575), which would unlock more funds to support local recovery efforts. Donald Trump denied that request last month, abandoning Western Maryland in its moment of need. Thankfully, following encouragement from Team Maryland, the federal government has made some resources available to farmers and small businesses through the [U.S. Department of Agriculture's (USDA) Farm Service Agency (FSA) Emergency Farm Loan program](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-alsobrooks-mcclain-delaney-encourage-western-maryland-farmers-to-seek-usda-recovery-assistance-in-wake-of-severe-flash-flooding&redir_log=176645121531343) and the [Small Business Administration](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fnews%252fpress-releases%252fvan-hollen-alsobrooks-mcclain-delaney-encourage-residents-to-seek-assistance-through-the-us-small-business-administration-in-wake-of-severe-flash-flooding%26redir_log%3d083502409911458&redir_log=109361302442718) Disaster Assistance program. But there is more to do. Maryland is appealing the denial of the disaster declaration for broader FEMA relief, [and the entire Maryland Congressional Delegation has sent a bipartisan letter in support of this appeal so that Western Maryland can get the funds it needs](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fin-bipartisan-push-maryland-congressional-delegation-urges-administration-to-reverse-denial-of-federal-disaster-assistance&redir_log=748159909871239).

As extreme weather becomes more intense and more frequent, the federal government should have our communities' backs. [I will fight tooth and nail](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fbsky.app%2fprofile%2fvanhollen.senate.gov%2fpost%2f3ltrrk3yxa226&redir_log=882903040341441) to stop this Administration from dismantling FEMA and throwing up bureaucratic red tape that delays life-saving disaster relief.

These are just some of the conversations I had with Marylanders recently. In every place I've visited, patriots are answering Trump's betrayal with solidarity. While [President Trump uses the Oval Office to cash in for himself, his family, and his donors](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d15812QQQ50759586QQQ7466002QQQ7578828%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fx.com%252fChrisVanHollen%252fstatus%252f1968343836687733173%26redir_log%3d653723861421332&redir_log=680255693441559), Marylanders are fighting for their neighbors. Together, we will save our democracy, our Constitution, and the country we love.

Onwards,
---
[Please click here to unsubscribe.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100064&__crop=15391.51032166.7465512.7517223)
|

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | September 22, 2025 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Senate Update: The Fall Fights Ahead Over the past eight months, Donald Trump and Republicans have made clear where their priorities lie. Instead of attempting to tackle rising costs, the Trump Administration is selling out working families to stuff the pockets of billionaires, special interests, and his own family. Americans are suffering the consequences, and our democracy is fraying. But across the country, more and more people are seeing through their lies, standing up, and fighting back--for an economy where everyone can afford a decent quality of life and for a democracy that preserves our freedoms, values, and rights. These are troubling, stormy, and downright terrifying times. Rising political violence has shocked our nation's conscience. The assassination of Charlie Kirk was a tragedy. Political violence has no place in our democracy. We must speak out for what we believe in vigorously, courageously, and peacefully. But that's not what the Trump Administration is doing. The President could have used this moment to unite the country. Instead, , including by using the FCC to try to drive Jimmy Kimmel off the air and to threaten others. , and I will keep fighting until all our rights are secure. , we must stand with those who stand up to Donald Trump's bullying and call out those who don't. In my remarks to the marchers, I called out ABC for caving to Trump when they didn't have to. I am glad ABC has reversed course and rehired Kimmel. Those who appease Trump only feed his appetite and put the rights of all of us at greater risk. Here's the latest. The One Big Ugly Betrayal Trump and Republicans are hoping that the American people don't learn the truth about what they call their "One Big Beautiful Bill." They jammed it through Congress so fast that many of them didn't even read it. Since so few of them seem to know the facts, I laid it out clearly on the . You can learn more about how the bill will affect Marylanders and all Americans--including the inconvenient truths Republicans are trying to hide--by reading this . Did you know Republicans are now trying to rebrand their bill? That's because the American people are learning more and more about what's in it--and they don't like what they see. The Republicans don't want to call their law the "Big Beautiful Bill" anymore because the American people can tell that it's only "beautiful" if you're a billionaire or a very wealthy person who is getting a windfall tax break. To help pay for those tax giveaways to the richest of the rich, their bill slashes health care, food assistance, and energy programs that working families depend on. Millions will lose health insurance. Hospitals and nursing homes across the country will close. One study estimates that the bill's health care cuts could result in 51,000 preventable deaths every year. And they left in place a ticking time bomb: at the end of this year, many of the Affordable Care Act tax credits will expire. When that happens, not only will 4 million of our fellow Americans lose health care and become uninsured, but nearly 20 million Americans will see a huge spike in their premiums. to discuss these devastating cuts to our social safety net with service and health providers that see their impact each day. They know more than anyone the ways that this bill will hurt families. And even with all these cuts, the bill still adds trillions to the national debt to pay for their tax cuts for billionaires. Once again, Republicans in Congress did not have the guts to stand up to Donald Trump--they were his rubber stamps. They made clear where their values lie--supporting the ultra-rich at the expense of everyone else. what is known as the "rescissions process," rescinding previously agreed-to funds. That bill eliminated what had been bipartisan funding for public television and public radio because Donald Trump doesn't like news stations that provide the facts or tell the truth. Their rescissions bill also kneecapped our foreign assistance efforts to combat public health risks and advance American interests abroad. Under Trump and Congressional Republicans, the billionaires and our foreign adversaries win; everyone else loses. Now the Trump Administration is going even further, pursuing a lawless effort to cancel funding--that has been approved by Congress and signed into law--without even a vote in Congress. Specifically, Trump's Office of Management and Budget Director, Russell Vought, terminated $5 billion in Congressionally appropriated funding and said that the cancellation would stand even if Congress did not vote on it. This maneuver, called a "pocket rescission," is blatantly unconstitutional. As the Constitution makes clear and the independent, nonpartisan Government Accountability Office has reaffirmed, the President of the United States does not have the power to unilaterally cancel laws that are already on the books. The Looming Trump Shutdown The next major fight before us is over keeping the federal government open. Congressional Republicans have surrendered their constitutional duties to Trump. They have become his rubber stamps. He is the only one calling the shots for Republicans, and he had publicly declared that he would not work with Democrats to fund the government. Only after we blocked his blank check House Republican bill in the Senate on Friday did he finally agree to meet with Democratic leaders to discuss the way forward. I, for one, will not vote to give Donald Trump a blank check to pursue his extreme and lawless agenda. There are no adequate safeguards in the Republicans' proposal to prevent him from continuing to illegally withhold and transfer public funds at his whim. Without those guardrails, it's not worth the paper it's written on. Moreover, there is nothing to defuse the ticking time bomb he left on Americans' health care that will explode at the end of the year and result in millions of Americans losing access to care and huge spikes in premiums for others. Trump has refused to work with us to address these and other issues. He has chosen instead to send us barreling toward a shutdown. I explained all of this in the last few days--see more . Combatting Trump's Authoritarian Overreach Trump's quest for absolute power is corroding our judicial and democratic institutions. As discussed earlier, he is doubling down on his attacks on free speech. . He is creating a culture of fear throughout the government for anyone who presents facts that don't fit his political narrative. These pressures are corrupting our institutions. His politicization of the National Guard is another abuse of power. I have worked successfully over the last few years to increase the presence of FBI, DEA, and ATF agents in Baltimore to work in coordination with Mayor Scott and local law enforcement. As a result of these and other measures taken in partnership with the Mayor and others, we have seen important progress in strengthening public safety. There is certainly more work ahead, and we always welcome additional federal resources to support local efforts, but unilaterally deploying the National Guard there -- as Trump has threatened -- is not the answer. The Posse Comitatus Act states--and a federal district court judge recently affirmed--that the president is barred from deploying the military for domestic law enforcement. Trump's real objective is clear: trying to normalize the idea that -- despite the law -- he can do whatever he wants with the military. Beyond deploying the National Guard, Trump also partially took over DC's Metropolitan Police Department, and he tried to seize total control until a federal judge warned that she would block such a move. He has also diverted important federal law enforcement resources away from national priorities--like fighting drug kingpins and going after the nation's most wanted criminals--to engage in traffic stops in DC neighborhoods. If Republicans wanted to address public safety in the District of Columbia, they wouldn't have blocked a billion dollars of the District's own local funding for police, firefighters, schools, and other essential services earlier this year. Senator Collins and I passed a measure in the Senate to allow DC to implement its plan to use its own tax monies for these purposes, but House Republicans have not taken up our bill. Trump's actions in DC also highlight another issue--the lack of self-determination for the people who reside in our nation's capital. Since 2020, I've been working to advance legislation that would give DC control over its own National Guard and revoke the president's ability to federalize the DC police force. Ultimately, the answer is to give the people of the District of Columbia full voting representation in the House and Senate by granting them statehood. I am the Senate's lead sponsor of the bill to do that. It is a stain on our democracy that the people of DC, the capital of our country, with a larger population than two states, don't have full representation and voting rights in the House and Senate. Seeking the Truth and Fighting for Humanity in Israel, Gaza, and the West Bank As I have long said, Israel was not only justified, but had a duty to its people, to defend itself against Hamas, which murdered over 1,200 people and seized over 250 hostages on October 7. We must not rest until every hostage comes home. There must be no more October 7s. But the extremist Netanyahu government has gone far beyond targeting Hamas to imposing collective punishment on all the people of Gaza. The situation in Gaza has gone from horrible to hell on earth. As the Netanyahu government restricts food, . This latest stage of food deprivation began on March 2, when the Netanyahu government imposed a total blockade for 78 days on all food and other humanitarian assistance entering Gaza. Then, Netanyahu pushed out the relatively effective, UN-backed aid delivery infrastructure and replaced it, in part, with the so-called Gaza Humanitarian Foundation (GHF), a private group backed by $30 million from the Trump Administration. The head of the GHF quit immediately upon its launch because, as he said, "it is not possible to implement this plan while also strictly adhering to the humanitarian principles of humanity, neutrality, impartiality, and independence, which I will not abandon." The GHF was allowed to establish only four sites--three of them concentrated in southern Gaza and none in the north--to feed over 2 million people. That replaced the UN and World Food Program system that had previously served about 400 sites throughout Gaza. Recently, I traveled to the Middle East with Senator Jeff Merkley, where we investigated these and other issues firsthand. We visited Israel, the West Bank, Jordan, and Egypt, including points along the border with Gaza. During our trip, we also spoke with government officials, local communities, international humanitarian aid organizations, U.S. personnel, civil society organizations, and more. One of our first meetings of the trip took place in in Tel Aviv, where we sat down with families whose loved ones were taken hostage by Hamas in their heinous terrorist attack on October 7. The families we spoke with all told us that Netanyahu has placed his own political survival over the survival of the hostages. We also visited , the site of one of the worst massacres on October 7. The thriving kibbutz we had visited before the war, in the summer of 2023, has become a much emptier and somber place. But the community remains strong, and a community leader there gave us the same message we received from other hostage families: end the war, return the hostages, and find a way for Israelis and Palestinians to live in peace with security, dignity, and human rights--including the right of self-determination--for all. We also visited communities in the , where we discussed the increasing, ongoing violence by extremist settlers, including meeting with American citizens whose family members have been killed by the IDF and violent settlers. We also met with members of the Christian community of Taybeh, which was recently attacked by violent settlers. We closely examined the very broken system for the distribution of humanitarian aid to Gaza, including visiting the and border crossings into Gaza and the Jordan aid corridor. What we learned firsthand reinforced the reports of devastation, starvation, and the Netanyahu government's use of hunger as a weapon of war as it pursues its openly stated current war aim of removing Palestinians from Gaza. We recently issued a full report on our findings, titled I have been very deliberate in distinguishing between the actions of the extremist Netanyahu government and the people of Israel--in the same way that I believe Americans have a duty to stand up against the extremist actions of Donald Trump. We have a duty to ensure that our tax dollars do not support actions by the Netanyahu government that violate our laws and our values. That's why I have consistently . America has leverage. We must use that leverage to demand adherence to American law and American values. We must demand a ceasefire, a return of all hostages, and a flood of humanitarian aid into Gaza NOW. Action in Our Communities Over the last few months, I have visited communities across Maryland to discuss their priorities, to stand up to Trump's abuses, and speak with people about how, together, we're fighting back against these betrayals. Senator Alsobrooks and Representatives Mfume, Ivey, Olszewski, Elfreth, and I attempted to visit the Baltimore ICE facility where the Trump Administration is holding people in inhumane conditions before working to deport them. . We told them that denying us the opportunity to inspect the facility was a violation of the law, and I read that very provision in law to them. They later relented, and Representatives Mfume, Elfreth, and I returned to inspect the facility. The rooms are only equipped for 12-hour holds, but ICE is detaining people there for much longer. ICE would not let us speak to the detainees to learn directly from them about their experiences or conditions. If ICE had nothing to hide, they wouldn't be throwing up roadblocks at every point. Here's the truth: Trump is not fixing our broken immigration system. He said his Administration would focus on the "worst of the worst," but--according to ICE's own data from this summer--84% of ICE's detainees pose no public safety risk. ICE has since removed that data from its dashboard, so the public can no longer see it. They want to hide what they're doing to people in ICE facilities from the American people; we can't let them get away with that. Our immigration system is broken and needs to be fixed. That's why I support strong border security, removing the worst of the worst, and improving our legal immigration system. But we must say no to mass deportation, no to tearing families apart, no to concentration camps, and no to surrendering the right of due process. We will not snuff out Lady Liberty's flame. , an American citizen and Baltimore resident, who the Venezuelan government wrongfully detained for seven months. I worked quietly with a bipartisan team of Senators, the nonprofit hostage advocacy group the Foley Foundation, and others to help secure his release. I'm grateful to the special envoy and our partners in that effort, and I will keep working for the liberty of EVERY American wrongfully detained. . We've heard from Marylanders, both individual taxpayers and business owners, waiting years for refunds they're owed. The Trump Administration's mass staff cuts at the IRS have likely contributed to these processing delays. On the Eastern Shore, I held a town hall with over 400 Marylanders at Chesapeake College. I took questions from residents on a range of topics, from the threats to our democracy to ways we work together for the Eastern Shore and our state. . From there, I spent time with our local leaders at the annual conference of the Maryland Association of Counties (MACo), joined the Salisbury Area and Greater Ocean City Chambers of Commerce for a forum to discuss Eastern Shore priorities, and also visited the , where Trump canceled a $36 million FEMA grant designed to harden the community's resilience against floods that regularly cause damage to local residents' homes and businesses. . I'm also continuing to support the recovery effort after the floods that devastated Allegany and Garrett Counties this spring. , which would unlock more funds to support local recovery efforts. Donald Trump denied that request last month, abandoning Western Maryland in its moment of need. Thankfully, following encouragement from Team Maryland, the federal government has made some resources available to farmers and small businesses through the and the Disaster Assistance program. But there is more to do. Maryland is appealing the denial of the disaster declaration for broader FEMA relief, . As extreme weather becomes more intense and more frequent, the federal government should have our communities' backs. to stop this Administration from dismantling FEMA and throwing up bureaucratic red tape that delays life-saving disaster relief. These are just some of the conversations I had with Marylanders recently. In every place I've visited, patriots are answering Trump's betrayal with solidarity. While , Marylanders are fighting for their neighbors. Together, we will save our democracy, our Constitution, and the country we love. Onwards, |

name: Scott Fitzgerald
subject: Revival of American Values
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Fitzgerald for Congress
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/e222081806b4/be-the-difference-today-45998?e=561a28864e)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=e35c2f7f46&e=561a28864e)
| | | | |
---
| |

Dear Friend,
Last week on the House floor, I proudly stood with my Republican colleagues to advance legislation that defends our freedoms, reins in wasteful spending, and holds Washington accountable to the American people.
One of the most meaningful moments was the vote on the **Charlie Kirk resolution where we** honored his life and condemned political violence. While a strong bipartisan majority stood tall and voted for it, I was disappointed to see far too many Democrats try to turn this into a partisan issue. At a time when we should be united against violence and in defense of free speech, they chose politics over principle. That should outrage every American.
But I believe we are in the middle of a **revival of American values,** a renewed commitment to faith, family, freedom, and the Constitution. It’s this spirit that gives me hope and strengthens my resolve to fight every day for the people I represent.
Despite their obstruction, I’ve remained focused on the fights of protecting your tax dollars, defending the Constitution, and securing our borders. Patriotism isn’t just a word, it’s action.
But make no mistake, the Left will continue doing everything they can to weaken our nation, erase our traditions, and put America last. I refuse to let that happen. Together, we can keep the flame of liberty burning bright for the next generation.
Your support makes it possible for me to stay in the fight. [If you’re able, would you consider making a contribution today?](https://scottfitzgeraldforcongress.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c59bc0db5e945d61b14749b2b&id=86c517947f&e=561a28864e) Every gift helps us defend our conservative values and keep America strong.
Thank you for standing with me and for standing up for America.
Onward,
Congressman Scott Fitzgerald

---
| |
Paid for by Scott Fitzgerald for Congress
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Dear Friend, Last week on the House floor, I proudly stood with my Republican colleagues to advance legislation that defends our freedoms, reins in wasteful spending, and holds Washington accountable to the American people. One of the most meaningful moments was the vote on the Charlie Kirk resolution where we honored his life and condemned political violence. While a strong bipartisan majority stood tall and voted for it, I was disappointed to see far too many Democrats try to turn this into a partisan issue. At a time when we should be united against violence and in defense of free speech, they chose politics over principle. That should outrage every American. But I believe we are in the middle of a revival of American values, a renewed commitment to faith, family, freedom, and the Constitution. It’s this spirit that gives me hope and strengthens my resolve to fight every day for the people I represent. Despite their obstruction, I’ve remained focused on the fights of protecting your tax dollars, defending the Constitution, and securing our borders. Patriotism isn’t just a word, it’s action. But make no mistake, the Left will continue doing everything they can to weaken our nation, erase our traditions, and put America last. I refuse to let that happen. Together, we can keep the flame of liberty burning bright for the next generation. Your support makes it possible for me to stay in the fight. Every gift helps us defend our conservative values and keep America strong. Thank you for standing with me and for standing up for America. Onward, Congressman Scott Fitzgerald | | Paid for by Scott Fitzgerald for Congress | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2025 Scott Fitzgerald for Congress. All rights reserved.

body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Donald Trump is politicizing our troops and turning them into his private army.

He’s illegally sent National Guard members to cities across the country and is threatening to send them to even more.

First, he sent troops to D.C. Now he’s sending them to Memphis. Trump is claiming that he’s fighting crime, but we know this is nothing more than playing politics with our military.

Jason served our country in uniform, fighting three combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan. While Trump is using our troops as political pawns, Jason will always fight for our servicemembers. Will you pitch in right now and stand with Jason? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-sep25]

name: James Talarico
subject: I’ve made a decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
After a great deal of reflection and prayer, I’ve made a decision...

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115725398/571260871/1901600101?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.22_James-Talarico-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODY0ZmYzNS1mZDk3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5W88ALp3YO1Lz45lAnLQgrb7Otfv5JxYQaeGnHdB2ss=) I’ve heard from a lot of Texans this summer. And after a great deal of reflection and prayer, I’ve made a decision:

I’m running for the U.S. Senate.

**_[I’m asking you to make one of the first donations to my campaign. Split it between my campaign and Maggie Hassan. This is a massive undertaking, and it would mean the world to me to know you have my back.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115725399/571260872/1901600101?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.22_James-Talarico-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODY0ZmYzNS1mZDk3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5W88ALp3YO1Lz45lAnLQgrb7Otfv5JxYQaeGnHdB2ss=)_** | | | [CHIP IN $10 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115725400/571260873/1901600101?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.22_James-Talarico-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODY0ZmYzNS1mZDk3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5W88ALp3YO1Lz45lAnLQgrb7Otfv5JxYQaeGnHdB2ss=)
---
Right now, there’s something broken in America. Our economy is broken. Our politics are broken. Even our relationships with each other feel broken.

**That’s because the most powerful people in the world want it that way.**

The biggest divide in America is not left versus right — it’s top versus bottom.

Billionaires want us looking left and right at each other instead of looking up at them.

The people at the top work so hard to keep us angry and divided because our unity is a threat to their wealth and power. So their cable news networks and their social media algorithms tear us apart.

They divide us by party, by race, by gender, by religion so we don’t notice they’re defunding our schools, gutting our healthcare, and cutting taxes for themselves and their rich friends.

It’s the oldest strategy in the world: divide and conquer. But we will not be conquered.

**The fight ahead will not be easy. We’re the underdogs.**

We’re going up against these billionaire mega-donors and their puppet politicians. We’re going up against a rigged system. And we’re going up against a lot of money.

But I’m a former middle school teacher — I don’t scare easily. And Texans don’t scare easily.

**Big Money is powerful, but it's nothing compared to people power.**

That’s why this is going to be a campaign fueled by people like you. I’ve never taken a dime of corporate PAC money, and I refuse to start now. So I need you with me.

**_[Please split a contribution between my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Maggie Hassan today to become a Founding Donor. We need as many people as possible chipping in on day one to prove our movement is strong enough.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115725401/571260874/1901600101?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.22_James-Talarico-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_IA_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODY0ZmYzNS1mZDk3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5W88ALp3YO1Lz45lAnLQgrb7Otfv5JxYQaeGnHdB2ss=)_**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to James Talarico and Maggie Hassan will go through immediately:_

clean_body: After a great deal of reflection and prayer, I’ve made a decision... = I’ve heard from a lot of Texans this summer. And after a great deal of reflection and prayer, I’ve made a decision: I’m running for the U.S. Senate. = | | | = Right now, there’s something broken in America. Our economy is broken. Our politics are broken. Even our relationships with each other feel broken. That’s because the most powerful people in the world want it that way. The biggest divide in America is not left versus right — it’s top versus bottom. Billionaires want us looking left and right at each other instead of looking up at them. The people at the top work so hard to keep us angry and divided because our unity is a threat to their wealth and power. So their cable news networks and their social media algorithms tear us apart. They divide us by party, by race, by gender, by religion so we don’t notice they’re defunding our schools, gutting our healthcare, and cutting taxes for themselves and their rich friends. It’s the oldest strategy in the world: divide and conquer. But we will not be conquered. The fight ahead will not be easy. We’re the underdogs. We’re going up against these billionaire mega-donors and their puppet politicians. We’re going up against a rigged system. And we’re going up against a lot of money. But I’m a former middle school teacher — I don’t scare easily. And Texans don’t scare easily. Big Money is powerful, but it's nothing compared to people power. That’s why this is going to be a campaign fueled by people like you. I’ve never taken a dime of corporate PAC money, and I refuse to start now. So I need you with me. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to James Talarico and Maggie Hassan will go through immediately: | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = We're going to make history, and we're going to do it together. | | Florida just did what every state in America should have done long ago: they're removing vaccine mandates for school kids. _This is a victory for medical freedom._

No government should ever force a medical decision on you, your children, or your family. **Every person deserves the right to choose what 's best for their health without bureaucrats looking over their shoulder.**

---
| Florida has a massive crisis, Peter...and Ron DeSantis and his fellow Republicans _refuse_ to address it.

Property insurance rates are _skyrocketing_ , with some paying _four times_ the national average. But that’s not all, Peter. The folks who aren’t getting price-gouged? They’re getting their policies _canceled._

**This is a _direct_ result of years of failed leadership from Ron DeSantis and his Republican legislature. He’s _failing_ to regulate the insurance industry, _failing_ to address the climate crisis, and _failing_ to do what is right for _all_ Floridians. **

While they refuse to act, I’m committed to actually showing up. I’m here in Congress, fighting for Floridians who have been left behind, doing what I can to right that wrong and make real change. But I can’t hold this seat without your help. [Somehow, the only thing the far-right in Florida can manage to do is spend millions to unseat me. I need your help to hold them off, so please, will you donate $27 to my campaign right now so we can actually address the problems that working families face? >>](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/LT3FwHjuUOYbJ1ybD5Vnqw~~/AAPPABA~/UBW1-Cyixd3Jd16cHddL3zXvXdLwWorl45LHl80Si8b36qpvHGmLbUK2rt8-IzSUGyjxOIy_yxr89DcVvGD4PWPIjB_9ZReMPb-2F2losP7C2FP1t50NJqCgtl9G3xhccP-uVJckpZ32_fhE8_vonlMy9n-lgE4Xh17wEKiqMZqAUtp9ezNxck0IH38ITvn2aZ00JjtMTvwNKFXVzzqfQMTbIqYWBF593FCV7xK4ttfHiZPG4IUmgY9GRaFQo8hNCGaSZ3ul8vqsCOVYVFXZ-DgrN9VC_MEZG0yrRTARm36P_iiKmgYMXSKFh0Do7zspL7nSbvpSffryqhdocFflt-E3dl4clkZnhUzMuyY-iXc~) | [DONATE NOW ➔](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/Z63C3XiZlgUB0OwHJRTuyg~~/AAPPABA~/UBW1-Cyixd3Jd16cHddL3zXvXdLwWorl45LHl80Si8b36qpvHGmLbUK2rt8-IzSUPZg3HN3oAnadPWLqv7e2hGKhoSwnjlHGOJYg39S4NFSntOjlEoF9sd900gwicWp6ll84d2bd3jPspWmK-rGXRWfkFuCaV0q5hgQBFJsxa7ephar9uojbwlAq_l52QjR8_n9LgRBe7ruPq1yReWSUgSxWXiw8CiIzY8--R099T71gxga_LbI1QSJrp021bwFd1ZTGQ3We7km-2SsX6EIsnRBL9ayzeOmQYks695PIRanE3CsXCMFuwQVSK6gDEg1YOVjSqFNozAUGZoXAnTT4r1-L8juzyMC6lPmNYYzayPY~)
---
Peter, this is personal to DeSantis. He has taken _millions_ from the insurance industry to fund his campaigns. Obviously that’s not somebody we can trust to regulate the industry. No, he’s putting their properties first – _over_ the needs of his actual constituents and voters.

My organizing team and I are working year-round to defeat this continued failed leadership. And I’m fighting hard to stop it at the source, introducing the bills we need in Congress to help everybody.

**But I’m up against millions in special interest money, and cannot defeat it without help from the grassroots. That’s you, Peter.**

clean_body: | ~ | Florida has a massive crisis, Peter...and Ron DeSantis and his fellow Republicans refuse to address it. Property insurance rates are skyrocketing , with some paying four times the national average. But that’s not all, Peter. The folks who aren’t getting price-gouged? They’re getting their policies canceled. This is a direct result of years of failed leadership from Ron DeSantis and his Republican legislature. He’s failing to regulate the insurance industry, failing to address the climate crisis, and failing to do what is right for all Floridians. While they refuse to act, I’m committed to actually showing up. I’m here in Congress, fighting for Floridians who have been left behind, doing what I can to right that wrong and make real change. But I can’t hold this seat without your help. ~ | ~ Peter, this is personal to DeSantis. He has taken millions from the insurance industry to fund his campaigns. Obviously that’s not somebody we can trust to regulate the industry. No, he’s putting their properties first – over the needs of his actual constituents and voters. My organizing team and I are working year-round to defeat this continued failed leadership. And I’m fighting hard to stop it at the source, introducing the bills we need in Congress to help everybody. But I’m up against millions in special interest money, and cannot defeat it without help from the grassroots. name: Wes Moore
subject: As I head to M&T Bank Stadium
body:
I can’t wait to decompress and watch the Ravens game tonight.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)
Peter, the Ravens are taking the field for Monday Night Football, and the whole city feels electric.
I can _smell_ the fresh-cut grass at M&T Bank Stadium. Thankfully, I’ll be there soon enough...
A lot of folks know me as a football guy. Maybe we could even coin the term ‘football governor.’ The funny thing is, I actually started my football career somewhat late in life.
I was at Johns Hopkins playing pickup _basketball_ when a coach approached me and asked me to run a few routes. Within a week, I’d gone from point guard to wide receiver.
Something about being out on that field just clicked. I felt at home — surrounded by teammates, part of a brotherhood, all working toward something bigger than ourselves.
These days I watch (a lot) more football than I play, and today’s game is a perfect chance to get in the mix, cheer on the team alongside some fellow fans, and — while it might not sound that relaxing — decompress.
It’s been a whirlwind month: We officially launched our campaign for re-election. It’s been full of media hits, planning sessions, and meeting with folks across the state. Suffice it to say, I’m ready to watch the Ravens bring home a win.
Winning a campaign is actually a lot like winning a football game. You need a solid game plan — and the right team. We’ve definitely got the right playbook:
\- We have amazing coaches (our campaign manager and all the great staff).
\- We have the playbook (talking about our record and agenda).
\- We have the shiny, new uniforms (I hear you can [buy our new shirts online](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/399?t=2&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)).
**Now we need to build the right team.** Before I head to M&T Bank Stadium, I’m asking you to join us. Your contribution powers the organizing, outreach, and momentum we need to execute our game plan and win for Maryland.
**[Add your $5 donation right now if you’re bought into our game plan and ready to bring this team to victory in the next election.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250922-2?refcode=em-20250922-2&refcode2=654_1086043_pPv1hF&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250922-2?amount=5&refcode=em-20250922-2&refcode2=654_1086043_pPv1hF&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)**
Thank you for everything these past few weeks — the love and support have been incredible. I appreciate each and every one of you.
Let’s go, Flock!
Wes

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20250922-2&t=3&refcode2=654_1086043_pPv1hF&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=654%2E1086043%2EpPv1hF)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

clean_body: Two years in Richmond, zero accomplishments for Virginians.| | | | | Hi Peter, | | As a lobbyist, Mark Earley, Jr., led the crusade to force pregnant women to undergo painful, invasive, and unnecessary medical procedures.Earley wants to completely ban abortion in all cases and even said that rape victims should be forced to give birth. | = = | | In two years as a legislator, Earley has failed to pass even a single piece of legislation and is ranked dead last in effectiveness because he’s more focused on banning abortion than engaging in real change to push Virginia forward. Earley even voted against Gov. Youngkin’s legislative priorities like funding law enforcement and schools because they weren’t extreme enough.When I represent District 73, I will work across the aisle to do what’s best for Virginia, not for lobbyists and special interest groups. It’s time to vote forVirginia’s interests,notEarley’s special interests. is an extremely close race, and every dollar counts. Ready to rush a donation through? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. | | = | = | | = | = | | Thank you for your support. I look forward to working for you when we win!Leslie | | = | | | ---|---|--- Paid For ByLeslie Mehta for Delegate PO Box 1721 Midlothian, VA 23113 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . Team, we’re about to face the first significant test of our campaign’s financial strength.

At the end of this month, we will be filing the first of our quarterly fundraising reports with the FEC.

You know what this means by now: Our opponent and his national Republican allies will soon see how much we’ve raised — they’ll see any drop in our fundraising numbers as evidence of weakness, and they’ll determine what they need to spend against us this fall and beyond based on that number.

[**Our end-of-quarter online goal is $555,000, and I’m reaching out personally to ask if you can start us off by splitting a donation of anything that is right for you between my team and Lisa Blunt Rochester today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115725665/571261782/1286950077?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.22_Cooper-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjBhNWNjOS1mZTk3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l0vw0gYHp1WrGVYeuSYxPJITiANhlSowdBprpwhzg4I=)

Hi, it's Shawn Harris -- the Democrat running to defeat Marjorie Taylor Greene in 2026. I'm running for Congress because northwest Georgia deserves a representative who shows up for people -- not sound bites. But my opponent, Marjorie Taylor Greene, keeps proving she's more interested in performative outrage than solving problems. Last week, ABC suspended Jimmy Kimmel after Trump pressured them to silence free speech. It's a dangerous move that jeopardizes our constitution. **But instead of defending basic freedoms, MTG rushed to play politics -- using the controversy to score points in Congress and posture online, just like she's done for years in her feud with Kimmel. **

clean_body: | | | Hi, it's Shawn Harris -- the Democrat running to defeat Marjorie Taylor Greene in 2026. I'm running for Congress because northwest Georgia deserves a representative who shows up for people -- not sound bites. But my opponent, Marjorie Taylor Greene, keeps proving she's more interested in performative outrage than solving problems. Last week, ABC suspended Jimmy Kimmel after Trump pressured them to silence free speech. It's a dangerous move that jeopardizes our constitution. But instead of defending basic freedoms, MTG rushed to play politics -- using the controversy to score points in Congress and posture online, just like she's done for years in her feud with Kimmel. | It 's a distraction from the real work our district needs. Here 's the choice: We can keep a headline-chasing culture warrior who lives for the next viral clip -- or we can elect a representative who'll focus on jobs, rural hospitals, veterans, and lowering costs for working families. If you're ready to flip the script in GA-14, I need you by my side right now. Grassroots donations are how we build a campaign strong enough to beat MTG's machine. ~~ ~~ ~~ Thank you for standing up for free speech, decency, and northwest Georgia. -- Shawn Harris P.S. The stakes are bigger than one TV show. They 're about whether bullies with power get to decide what you're allowed to hear -- and whether our district has a representative who works for you. | ~~ "Facebook" ~ "X" \[BLUESKY\] "Bluesky" PAID FOR BY SHAWN FOR GEORGIA

name: Team WFP
subject: Fighting back against Trump’s rigged election maps
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
This could shape who controls Congress for the next decade. | | | Peter, Trump and his allies are trying to rig our democracy by redrawing congressional maps in Texas to lock in their power. If they succeed, it could shape who controls Congress for the next decade. **We need to fight back at the state level. That’s why, in California, we’re supporting Prop 50. It could be a blueprint for other states too.** In short, Prop 50 is a statewide ballot measure that would allow Gov. Gavin Newsom to redraw California’s congressional districts. If successful, Democrats could gain five new seats in the House and _nullify_ MAGA’s gerrymandering in Texas. **But this ballot measure can only succeed if the voters pass it on November 4th. And it’s on all of us to drum up support and spread the word — even from afar.** [On Tuesday, September 23 at 9pm ET / 6pm PT, we are coming together online for a Yes on Prop 50 Virtual Rally. And we want EVERYONE to attend — because if Trump gets his way, we’ll need a lot more help to offset his rigging of Congressional maps.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMZSmNHPDmfLBsYkb-vbS-Yngx6Rx0JXDQEu341nf823YwQsHQ2-uEimts_vsuaKCngawp_kZjz1v-I1aBVjaG2Ac49XEgEHW72dEDu2VuwLk-qEi6DUc5WYQCucfPFmF9k5DHTDJl2kseFkA9c2N5XI7Xt8aJxW4nCOd2KYzoVvp-qkJTUG21YrasEKVqiEE38tAXxqr4Azc3TWkmqiIdswPAA5MFRGaidAt8KAz6PRDqYj5X-H0m0R4G6OQASvtkT8S-wYcfVt-6JhQU6skL6Z7yxwoqLDsM71X7ewwWTfc/4k4/YAwWav8uSX-rF_ilblyUsg/h0/h001.LJxwRZ9Arrn7gSLMH9dPitAt2PNygkCCLO5Ao9mrVLA) [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMZSmNHPDmfLBsYkb-vbS-Yngx6Rx0JXDQEu341nf823YwQsHQ2-uEimts_vsuaKCngawp_kZjz1v-I1aBVjaG2DVQlVVNXiz-G24m5fIag5f-8RqRSbvic3Zp1ZHe3S3-d0m5Viz8-Nax6MwOFdpg6OIBcUi7jDSbztR77lLb4YC4M5G4z15imynW4y_pSIkCwuW6TTOFbck4bVPIqotAN4huqAYnwY7uwOE9kofDsIKxdGrn5nyN4DTYhhx9tLjeTgtoot2w316KEP80NVs7cTStTM5ycrs16nx-V6JDuyw/4k4/YAwWav8uSX-rF_ilblyUsg/h1/h001.MokhxGBruLfQku1iYziYMZ1_QDm-sfwwsxDMH5kGTxQ) **[RSVP »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMZSmNHPDmfLBsYkb-vbS-Yngx6Rx0JXDQEu341nf823YwQsHQ2-uEimts_vsuaKCngawp_kZjz1v-I1aBVjaG2DLDlR2zZgIiFoQWTTSpJ_8BX4wZ2ziA3tCDgKH1TKzMpQwJA5JYLKeI-ItHIt-gQ9SIJTZSu1MpZgGs9jEnZuS3hL5UoJ-jQKOAvgKV1Tk45bsgvWSCd_-ua4ZvNzOXeFLRzfqZcdVWFCKP4sAUXFqzjGUTHb9KdLrt1ETGnnjgNQb1s9KH0tBE-Mpp1ITgCc1pS9OS8eq0pQCE6VNLp07/4k4/YAwWav8uSX-rF_ilblyUsg/h2/h001.vE-AboaxDp4QIEC1ffA3TWI-9AkSV9OPMn1YFh2F_FQ)** At the virtual rally, you will hear from movement leaders including **Congresswoman Lateefah Simon, Lorena Gonzalez (President of the California Labor Federation), Christina Livingston (ACCE Action Executive Director and CA WFP Chair), and Jane Kim (CA WFP State Director)**. The rally will be facilitated by **Irene Kao (Executive Director of Courage California)**. Prop 50 is California’s way of making sure our voices still matter in Washington. By adopting a stronger congressional map, we can push back against Trump’s rigged lines in Texas and defend fair representation for our communities. It's about protecting our democracy and making sure Californians cannot be silenced while Trump and his allies try to tip the scales. This is more than a rally. It is about building the people power we need to win Prop 50, defending our democracy, and growing the Working Families Party all across the nation. [RSVP today to join the rally, and then take the next step to sign up to volunteer and become a dues-paying member of the Working Families Party. clean_body: This could shape who controls Congress for the next decade. | | | Peter, Trump and his allies are trying to rig our democracy by redrawing congressional maps in Texas to lock in their power. If they succeed, it could shape who controls Congress for the next decade. We need to fight back at the state level. That’s why, in California, we’re supporting Prop 50. It could be a blueprint for other states too. In short, Prop 50 is a statewide ballot measure that would allow Gov. Gavin Newsom to redraw California’s congressional districts. If successful, Democrats could gain five new seats in the House and nullify MAGA’s gerrymandering in Texas. But this ballot measure can only succeed if the voters pass it on November 4th. And it’s on all of us to drum up support and spread the word — even from afar. At the virtual rally, you will hear from movement leaders including Congresswoman Lateefah Simon, Lorena Gonzalez (President of the California Labor Federation), Christina Livingston (ACCE Action Executive Director and CA WFP Chair), and Jane Kim (CA WFP State Director). The rally will be facilitated by Irene Kao (Executive Director of Courage California). Prop 50 is California’s way of making sure our voices still matter in Washington. By adopting a stronger congressional map, we can push back against Trump’s rigged lines in Texas and defend fair representation for our communities. It's about protecting our democracy and making sure Californians cannot be silenced while Trump and his allies try to tip the scales. This is more than a rally. It is about building the people power we need to win Prop 50, defending our democracy, and growing the Working Families Party all across the nation. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via .
